<h1>Copilot CLI 잔여 교차 관심사 분석</h1>
<h2>개요</h2>
<p>Copilot CLI(GitHub Copilot Coding Agent CLI)는 dimensions 01-12에 포함되지 않는 교차 관심사들을 다수 보유하고 있다. 특히 <strong>7단계 인증 체계</strong>와 <strong>tree-sitter WASM 기반 코드 분석</strong> 엔진은 Copilot CLI의 핵심 차별점이다.</p>
<p><strong>분석 대상 소스:</strong> <code>/Users/jun/Developer/codex/151_copilot_cli/decoded/</code></p>
<pre><code>핵심 파일 맵:
├── 00_bootstrap/
│   └── 0000__get.js                    # 보안 제약 require, 진입점
├── 01_core/
│   ├── git/
│   │   └── remote_hosts.js             # Git 원격 저장소 감지 (ADO/GitHub)
│   ├── telemetry/
│   │   ├── skills_metrics.js           # 스킬 호출/로드 텔레메트리
│   │   ├── workspace_events.js         # 워크스페이스 체크포인트/파일 이벤트
│   │   ├── mcp_events.js              # MCP 서버 이벤트 추적
│   │   └── tool_sanitizer.js          # 도구 결과 PII 세정
│   ├── agent/                          # 에이전트 런타임
│   ├── prompt/                         # 프롬프트 조립
│   └── shell/                          # 셸 명령 실행
├── 06_auth/
│   ├── 1008__keytar-api-copilot.js     # 7-타입 인증 디스패치 + keytar
│   ├── 0728__identity-broker.js        # Copilot 토큰 브로커
│   ├── 1096__gists.js                  # Gist 기반 인증 저장
│   └── 1746__session-hooks.js          # 세션 인증 훅
├── 11_search/
│   ├── 0647__search-resources.js       # 검색 프롬프트 시스템
│   └── 1657__tree-sitter-wasm.js       # 17언어 tree-sitter WASM 하이라이팅
├── 12_worker/                          # 워커 스레드 관리
├── 90_native/                          # 네이티브 prebuild 모듈
└── 91_vendor/                          # 번들된 벤더 의존성
</code></pre>
<hr>
<h2>1. 부트스트랩 체인</h2>
<h3>1.1 보안 제약 <code>require</code> 시스템</h3>
<p><strong>파일 경로:</strong> <code>/Users/jun/Developer/codex/151_copilot_cli/decoded/00_bootstrap/0000__get.js</code></p>
<p>Copilot CLI 진입점은 <strong>import 경로 탈출을 방지</strong>하는 커스텀 <code>require</code> 함수를 정의한다. 이는 모든 <code>require</code> 호출이 애플리케이션 디렉토리 범위 내에서만 이루어지도록 강제한다.</p>
<pre><code class="language-javascript">// 0000__get.js — 보안 제약 require
const require = (module) => {
  let req = __rootRequire;

  // 특수 네이티브 모듈 라우팅
  if (typeof module === "string" &#x26;&#x26; module.startsWith("@img/")) {
    req = __sharpRequire;      // sharp 이미지 처리 라이브러리
  }
  if (typeof module === "string" &#x26;&#x26; module.startsWith("@teddyzhu/")) {
    req = __clipboardRequire;  // 클립보드 네이티브 바인딩
  }

  // Node.js 빌트인 모듈은 통과
  if (typeof module === "string" &#x26;&#x26; __module.isBuiltin(module)) {
    return req(module);
  }

  // 핵심 보안: 실제 경로가 앱 디렉토리 밖이면 차단
  const modulePath = __fs.realpathSync(req.resolve(module));
  const appPath = __fs.realpathSync(import.meta.dirname);
  const relativePath = __path.relative(appPath, modulePath);

  if (relativePath.startsWith("..")) {
    throw new Error(
      "Requiring module outside of application is a security concern; module: " +
        modulePath + ", app: " + appPath
    );
  }

  return req(module);
};
</code></pre>
<p><strong>설계 특징:</strong></p>
<ul>
<li><code>__fs.realpathSync</code>로 심볼릭 링크 우회 공격 방지</li>
<li><code>__path.relative</code>로 상대 경로가 <code>..</code>로 시작하면 즉시 차단</li>
<li><code>@img/sharp</code>와 <code>@teddyzhu/clipboard</code>에 대해 별도 <code>createRequire</code> 컨텍스트 제공</li>
</ul>
<h3>1.2 Webpack 모듈 시스템</h3>
<pre><code class="language-javascript">// 지연 초기화 팩토리 패턴
var W = (t, e) => () => (t &#x26;&#x26; (e = t((t = 0))), e);  // 일회성 팩토리
var k = (t, e) => () => (e || t((e = { exports: {} }).exports, e), e.exports);  // CommonJS 래퍼
</code></pre>
<p><strong>핵심 패턴:</strong></p>
<ul>
<li><code>W()</code>: 한 번 호출되면 팩토리 함수를 <code>0</code>으로 리셋하여 재호출 시 캐시된 결과 반환</li>
<li><code>k()</code>: CommonJS <code>module.exports</code> 패턴으로 모듈을 래핑하여 싱글턴 보장</li>
<li><code>Me()</code>: ESModule interop — <code>__esModule</code> 플래그 기반 default export 해결</li>
</ul>
<h3>1.3 부트스트랩 순서</h3>
<pre><code class="language-mermaid">flowchart TD
    A["0000__get.js (진입점)"] --> B[보안 require 설정]
    B --> C[Webpack 모듈 시스템 초기화]
    C --> D["1358___7r.js (86KB 핵심 런타임)"]
    D --> E[인증 로드]
    E --> F[MCP 서버 시작]
    F --> G[도구 등록]
    G --> H[TUI 렌더링 시작]

    subgraph "네이티브 모듈"
        I["@img/sharp (이미지)"]
        J["@teddyzhu/clipboard (클립보드)"]
        K["keytar.node (OS 키체인)"]
    end

<h2>2. 인증 / API 키 관리 (핵심 강점)</h2>
<h3>2.1 아키텍처 개요</h3>
<p>Copilot CLI는 <strong>7가지 인증 타입</strong>을 지원하며, 우선순위 기반으로 순차 탐색하는 체인 패턴을 구현한다. 이는 업계에서 가장 광범위한 인증 체계 중 하나이다.</p>
<p><strong>파일 경로:</strong> <code>/Users/jun/Developer/codex/151_copilot_cli/decoded/06_auth/1008__slash-keytar-build-api-copilot-api-github.js</code></p>
<pre><code class="language-mermaid">graph TD
    A[loadAuthInfo] --> B{SDK Token?}
    B -->|COPILOT_AUTH_TOKEN| C[SDK 토큰 검증]
    B -->|없음| D{HMAC?}
    D -->|CAPI_HMAC_KEY| E[HMAC 인증]
    D -->|없음| F{API Key?}
    F -->|ANTHROPIC/OPENAI_KEY| G[API 키 인증]
    F -->|없음| H{Copilot API Token?}
    H -->|GITHUB_COPILOT_API_TOKEN| I[Copilot API 토큰]
    H -->|없음| J{GitHub Token?}
    J -->|GH_TOKEN/GITHUB_TOKEN| K[GitHub PAT 검증]
    J -->|없음| L{Keytar 저장?}
    L -->|있음| M[키체인에서 토큰 조회]
    L -->|없음| N{gh CLI?}
    N -->|gh auth| O[gh CLI 토큰 추출]
    N -->|없음| P[인증 없음]

    style C fill:#4CAF50
    style E fill:#4CAF50
    style G fill:#8BC34A
    style I fill:#8BC34A
    style K fill:#FFC107
    style M fill:#FFC107
    style O fill:#FF9800
    style P fill:#f44336
</code></pre>
<h3>2.2 인증 타입 디스패치</h3>
<pre><code class="language-javascript">// Visitor 패턴 기반 인증 타입 디스패치
function a8(t, e) {
  switch (t.type) {
    case "hmac":             return e.onHMACAuthInfo(t);
    case "env":              return e.onEnvAuthInfo(t);
    case "user":             return e.onUserAuthInfo(t);
    case "gh-cli":           return e.onGhCliAuthInfo(t);
    case "api-key":          return e.onApiKeyAuthInfo(t);
    case "token":            return e.onTokenAuthInfo(t);
    case "copilot-api-token": return e.onCopilotApiTokenAuthInfo(t);
    default:
      throw new Error(`unexpected auth info type: ${JSON.stringify(t)}`);
  }
}
</code></pre>
<h3>2.3 우선순위 체인</h3>
<pre><code class="language-javascript">getPrioritizedAuthMethods(includeAllTokens) {
  let methods = [];
  // 1. SDK 토큰 (authTokenEnvVar 설정 시)
  if (this.config.authTokenEnvVar)
    methods.push(this.trySdkTokenLogin.bind(this));

  if (!this.config.disableAutoLogin) {
    methods.push(this.tryHMACLogin.bind(this));          // 2. HMAC
    methods.push(this.tryApiKeyLogin.bind(this));         // 3. API 키
    methods.push(this.tryCopilotApiTokenLogin.bind(this)); // 4. Copilot API
    methods.push(this.tryGitHubTokenLogin.bind(this));     // 5. PAT
    methods.push(                                           // 6. 키체인
      includeAllTokens
        ? this.getAllGitHubLoginTokens.bind(this)
        : this.tryLoginToken.bind(this)
    );
    methods.push(this.tryGhCliTokenLogin.bind(this));      // 7. gh CLI
  }
  return methods;
}
</code></pre>

<h3>2.5 토큰 저장 전략 (이중 경로)</h3>
<pre><code class="language-javascript">// 토큰 저장: keytar 우선, 실패 시 설정 파일 폴백
async storeToken(token, host, login, configPath) {
  if (!token) throw new Error("No token provided to store");
  let key = `${host}:${login}`;

  // 설정에서 평문 저장 모드 확인
  if ((await ir.load(configPath))?.store_token_plaintext)
    return (await storeCurrentTokenInConfig(host, login, configPath), true);

  // keytar 시도
  try {
    let keytar = await PQ();
    return (await jQ(keytar.setPassword("copilot-cli", key, token)), true);
  } catch {
    return false;  // keytar 실패 시 false 반환 (설정 파일 폴백 아님!)
  }
}
</code></pre>
<p><strong>설계 결정:</strong></p>
<ul>
<li><code>store_token_plaintext</code> 설정이 true면 설정 파일의 <code>copilot_tokens</code> 객체에 평문 저장</li>
<li>기본 경로: OS 키체인 (macOS Keychain, Windows Credential Vault, Linux Secret Service)</li>
<li><code>COPILOT_DISABLE_KEYTAR</code> 환경변수로 keytar 완전 비활성화 가능</li>
</ul>
<h3>2.6 GitHub PAT 토큰 검증</h3>
<pre><code class="language-javascript">async tryGitHubTokenLogin() {
  let token =
    process.env.COPILOT_GITHUB_TOKEN ||
    process.env.GH_TOKEN ||
    process.env.GITHUB_TOKEN ||
    this.getGitHubAskpassToken();  // GITHUB_ASKPASS 바이너리 실행

  // Classic PAT (ghp_) 차단 — 보안상 fine-grained PAT만 허용
  if (token.startsWith("ghp_")) {
    q.error("Classic PATs are not supported.");
    return [];
  }

  // 토큰 타입별 처리
  if (token.startsWith("ghs_"))   return [envAuth];       // GitHub App 설치 토큰
  if (token.startsWith("gho_"))   return [validateUser];   // OAuth 토큰
  if (token.startsWith("ghu_"))   return [validateUser];   // User-to-server 토큰
  if (token.startsWith("github_pat_")) return [validateUser]; // Fine-grained PAT
  return [];  // 알 수 없는 토큰 타입 무시
}
</code></pre>
<p><strong>GITHUB_ASKPASS 통합:</strong></p>
<pre><code class="language-javascript">getGitHubAskpassToken() {
  let askpass = process.env.GITHUB_ASKPASS;
  if (askpass) {
    let result = spawnSync(askpass, [], {
      encoding: "utf8",
      timeout: 10000,        // 10초 타임아웃
      maxBuffer: 1048576,    // 1MB 버퍼
    });
    return result.stdout?.trim();
  }
}
</code></pre>
<h3>2.7 Multi-provider API 키 지원</h3>
<pre><code class="language-javascript">async tryApiKeyLogin() {
  if (!x_()) return [];  // SDK 모드에서만 활성화

  if (process.env.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY)
    return [{ type: "api-key", apiKey: key, host: "https://api.anthropic.com" }];
  if (process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY)
    return [{ type: "api-key", apiKey: key, host: process.env.OPENAI_BASE_URL || "https://api.openai.com" }];
  if (process.env.AZURE_OPENAI_API_KEY)
    return [{ type: "api-key", apiKey: key, host: process.env.AZURE_OPENAI_API_ENDPOINT }];
}

<h3>2.8 GHE (GitHub Enterprise) 지원</h3>
<pre><code class="language-javascript">function T9s(host) {
  let url = host.startsWith("https://") ? host : `https://${host}`;

  // github.com → 공용 Copilot API
  if (_oe(url))
    return { endpoints: { api: "https://api.githubcopilot.com" } };

  // *.ghe.com → 기업 Copilot API
  let match = new URL(url).hostname.match(/^(.+)\.ghe\.com$/);
  if (match)
    return { endpoints: { api: `https://copilot-api.${match[1]}.ghe.com` } };

  return { endpoints: {} };
}
</code></pre>

<h2>3. Git/VCS 통합</h2>
<h3>3.1 원격 저장소 감지 엔진</h3>
<p><strong>파일 경로:</strong> <code>/Users/jun/Developer/codex/151_copilot_cli/decoded/01_core/git/remote_hosts.js</code></p>
<p>Copilot CLI는 **Azure DevOps(ADO)**와 <strong>GitHub</strong> 원격을 자동 감지하여 인증 및 API 엔드포인트를 결정한다.</p>
<pre><code class="language-javascript">async function FDr(cwd, featureFlags) {
  if (!featureFlags.ADO_DETECTION) return { hasAzureDevOps: false, hasGitHub: false };

  let { gitRoot, found } = await Ra(cwd);
  if (!found) return { hasAzureDevOps: false, hasGitHub: false };

  let remotes = await jq(gitRoot);  // git remote -v 파싱
  // origin 우선 정렬
  let prioritized = originFirst ? [originFirst, ...rest] : remotes;

  for (let remote of prioritized) {
    let urls = [remote.FetchURL, remote.PushURL].filter(Boolean);
    for (let url of urls) {
      let parsed = gitUrlParse(url);
      // Azure DevOps 감지: dev.azure.com, vs-ssh.visualstudio.com 등
      if (!azureUrl &#x26;&#x26; NEe(parsed.resource))
        azureUrl = url;
      // GitHub 감지: github.com, *.ghe.com
      if (!hasGitHub &#x26;&#x26; YDr(parsed.resource))
        hasGitHub = true;
    }
  }
  return { hasAzureDevOps: !!azureUrl, azureDevOpsUrl: azureUrl, hasGitHub };
}
</code></pre>
<p><strong>URL 파싱 폴백:</strong></p>
<pre><code class="language-javascript">// git URL 파서 실패 시 정규식 폴백
function xmi(url) {
  // HTTPS: https://host:port/path
  let httpsMatch = /^https?:\/\/([^/:?#]+)(?:\d+)?(?:[/?#]|$)/.exec(url);
  if (httpsMatch?.[1]) return httpsMatch[1];
  // SSH: user@host:path
  let sshMatch = /^[^@]+@([^:/\s]+):/.exec(url);
  if (sshMatch?.[1]) return sshMatch[1];
}
</code></pre>
<h3>3.2 Diff 엔진</h3>
<p><strong>파일 경로:</strong> <code>/Users/jun/Developer/codex/151_copilot_cli/decoded/11_search/1657__...highlights.js</code></p>
<p>Copilot CLI는 <code>git diff</code>와 <code>git status</code>를 조합하여 <strong>전체 변경사항 맵</strong>을 구축한다.</p>
<pre><code class="language-mermaid">flowchart TD
    A[kLe - 변경사항 수집] --> B{git HEAD 있음?}
    B -->|Yes| C["git diff HEAD --name-status"]
    B -->|No| D["git status --porcelain"]
    C --> E[파일별 diff 수집]
    E --> F{maxBuffer 초과?}
    F -->|Yes| G[isTruncated=true]
    F -->|No| H[diff 텍스트 저장]
    D --> I[Untracked 파일 감지]
    I --> J["?? 프리픽스 파일 읽기"]
    J --> K[합성 diff 생성]

    L["fco - 브랜치 기반 비교"] --> M{merge-base 계산}
    M --> N["remote/main..HEAD"]
    M -->|실패| O["main..HEAD"]
    O -->|실패| P[kLe 폴백]
    N --> Q[참조 기반 diff + 미추적 파일 병합]
</code></pre>

</thead>

<tr>

<td>5MB</td>
<td>파일 diff 최대 크기</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>maxBuffer</code></td>
<td>10MB</td>
<td>git 프로세스 출력 상한</td>

<p><strong>변경 타입 분류:</strong></p>
<pre><code class="language-javascript">function Aco(text) {
  let entries = new Map();
  for (let line of lines) {
    let status = line.split("\t")[0];
    if (status.startsWith("R"))  entries.set(path, { changeType: "renamed", oldPath });
    else if (status === "A")     entries.set(path, { changeType: "added" });
    else if (status === "D")     entries.set(path, { changeType: "deleted" });
    else                         entries.set(path, { changeType: "modified" });
  }
  return entries;
}
</code></pre>
<h3>3.3 Gist 가용성 검사</h3>
<pre><code class="language-javascript">function Dat(authInfo) {
  // GHE Cloud with data residency: Gist 불가
  if (authInfo.host.endsWith(".ghe.com"))
    return { supported: false, kind: "gheCloud",
             reason: "Gists are not available for GitHub Enterprise Cloud with data residency (*.ghe.com)." };

  // Enterprise Managed Users (EMU): Gist 불가
  if (_oe(authInfo.host) &#x26;&#x26; authInfo.login?.includes("_"))
    return { supported: false, kind: "emuUser",
             reason: "Gists are not available for Enterprise Managed Users." };

  return { supported: true };
}

<h2>4. 검색 엔진 — Tree-sitter WASM</h2>
<h3>4.1 아키텍처</h3>
<p>Copilot CLI는 <code>ripgrep</code> 외에 <strong>tree-sitter WASM</strong>을 사용한 구조적 코드 분석을 수행한다. 이는 CLI 에이전트 중 가장 정교한 코드 이해 인프라 중 하나이다.</p>
<pre><code class="language-mermaid">graph TD
    A[소스 파일] --> B{확장자 매칭}
    B --> C[언어별 WASM 모듈 로드]
    C --> D[tree-sitter Parser.parse]
    D --> E[highlight query 실행]
    E --> F[AST 캡처]
    F --> G[시맨틱 컬러 매핑]
    G --> H[TUI diff 렌더링]

    I["17개 WASM 바이너리"] -.-> C
    J["SCM query 파일"] -.-> E
</code></pre>
<h3>4.2 지원 언어 테이블</h3>
<pre><code class="language-javascript">const LANGUAGE_DEFINITIONS = [

<h3>4.3 시맨틱 캡처 → 컬러 매핑</h3>
<p>tree-sitter의 highlight query 캡처 이름을 TUI 렌더링용 컬러 이름으로 변환한다:</p>
<pre><code class="language-javascript">// 60+ 캡처 → 11개 컬러 카테고리 매핑 (발췌)
const CAPTURE_COLOR_MAP = new Map([
  ["keyword",              "syntaxKeyword"],
  ["keyword.function",     "syntaxKeyword"],
  ["keyword.operator",     "syntaxOperator"],
  ["string",               "syntaxString"],
  ["comment",              "syntaxComment"],
  ["function",             "syntaxFunction"],
  ["function.call",        "syntaxFunction"],
  ["type",                 "syntaxType"],
  ["variable",             "syntaxVariable"],
  ["variable.builtin",     "syntaxConstant"],
  ["number",               "syntaxNumber"],
  ["operator",             "syntaxOperator"],
  ["punctuation",          "syntaxPunctuation"],
  ["constant",             "syntaxConstant"],
  ["tag",                  "syntaxTag"],
  ["attribute",            "syntaxAttribute"],
]);

// 계층적 폴백: "keyword.return" → "keyword" → 찾을 때까지 축소
function Umn(captureName) {
  let name = captureName.startsWith("@") ? captureName.slice(1) : captureName;
  while (name) {
    let color = CAPTURE_COLOR_MAP.get(name);
    if (color) return color;
    let dotIndex = name.lastIndexOf(".");
    if (dotIndex === -1) break;
    name = name.slice(0, dotIndex);  // "keyword.return" → "keyword"
  }
}
</code></pre>
<h3>4.4 하이라이팅 파이프라인</h3>
<pre><code class="language-javascript">async function IGt(sourceCode, filePath) {
  // 안전 가드
  if (Pmn || !sourceCode) return [];          // 초기화 실패 시 비활성
  if (sourceCode.length > 100000) return [];  // 100KB 초과 파일 스킵

  let langDef = Mmn(filePath);               // 확장자 → 언어 매핑
  if (!langDef) return [];

  if (!(await fYi())) return [];             // tree-sitter 초기화 확인

  let language = await EYi(langDef);          // WASM 언어 로드 (캐시)
  let query = await WYi(langDef, language);   // highlight query 로드 (캐시)
  if (!query) return [];

  let parser = new SAe();
  try {
    parser.setLanguage(language);
    let tree = parser.parse(sourceCode);
    return wYi(tree, query, sourceCode);     // 캡처 → 행별 컬러 배열
  } finally {
    parser.delete();                         // WASM 메모리 해제
  }
}
</code></pre>
<h3>4.5 Diff 인라인 하이라이팅</h3>
<pre><code class="language-javascript">// 변경/삭제 행의 단어 수준 diff 계산
function bco(oldLine, newLine, offset) {
  // 공통 접두사/접미사 제거
  let commonPrefixLen = 0;
  while (commonPrefixLen &#x3C; min &#x26;&#x26; oldLine[commonPrefixLen] === newLine[commonPrefixLen])
    commonPrefixLen++;

  // 단어 경계까지 확장 (/[A-Za-z0-9_$]/)
  let wordStart = expandToWordBoundary(oldLine, commonPrefixLen);

  // 변경된 부분만 추출하여 del/add 프래그먼트 생성
  return { delFragments, addFragments, cost, hasRealChange };
}

// O(n*m) 비용의 최적 쌍 매칭 (최대 200행, 40,000 쌍)
const MAX_BLOCK_SIZE = 200;        // hco
const MAX_PAIR_PRODUCT = 40000;    // aYi
</code></pre>
<h3>4.6 SCM Query 위생 처리</h3>
<pre><code class="language-javascript">// Vim/Lua 전용 쿼리 프레디킷 제거 (표준 tree-sitter에서 미지원)
function ZYi(querySource) {
  return querySource.replace(
    /\(#(?:lua-match\?|not-lua-match\?|vim-match\?|gsub!|set!|has-ancestor\?|not-has-ancestor\?|has-parent\?|not-has-parent\?|contains\?)[^)]*\)/g,
    "",
  );
}
</code></pre>

<h2>5. 텔레메트리 / 관측성</h2>
<h3>5.1 스킬 메트릭</h3>
<p><strong>파일 경로:</strong> <code>/Users/jun/Developer/codex/151_copilot_cli/decoded/01_core/telemetry/skills_metrics.js</code></p>
<p>스킬 호출과 로드를 추적하는 세분화된 텔레메트리 시스템이다.</p>
<pre><code class="language-javascript">// 스킬 호출 이벤트
function kXr(skillInvocation) {
  return {
    kind: "skill_invoked",
    properties: {
      event_id: skillInvocation.id,
      skill_name_hash: Va(skillInvocation.data.name),  // 이름 해시화 (PII 보호)
      has_allowed_tools: String(!!skillInvocation.data.allowedTools?.length),
    },
    restrictedProperties: {     // 내부 분석용 (외부 미전송)
      skill_name: skillInvocation.data.name,
      skill_path: skillInvocation.data.path,
      plugin_name: skillInvocation.data.pluginName,
      plugin_version: skillInvocation.data.pluginVersion,
    },
    metrics: {
      skill_content_length: skillInvocation.data.content.length,
      allowed_tools_count: skillInvocation.data.allowedTools?.length ?? 0,
    },
  };
}
</code></pre>
<h3>5.2 스킬 로드 메트릭</h3>
<pre><code class="language-javascript">// 스킬 로드 시 소스별 분류 집계
function LXr(loadResult) {
  let sourceCounts = {
    project: 0,                 // 프로젝트 로컬 스킬
    inherited: 0,               // 상위에서 상속
    "personal-copilot": 0,      // Copilot 개인 스킬
    "personal-claude": 0,       // Claude 개인 스킬
    plugin: 0,                  // 플러그인 스킬
    custom: 0,                  // 커스텀 스킬
  };

  let commandCount = 0,
      userInvocableCount = 0,
      modelInvocationDisabledCount = 0;

  for (let skill of loadResult.skills) {
    sourceCounts[skill.source]++;
    if (skill.isCommand) commandCount++;
    if (skill.userInvocable) userInvocableCount++;
    if (skill.disableModelInvocation) modelInvocationDisabledCount++;
  }

  return {
    kind: "skills_loaded",
    metrics: {
      total_skill_count: loadResult.skills.length,
      project_skill_count: sourceCounts.project,
      inherited_skill_count: sourceCounts.inherited,
      personal_skill_count: sourceCounts["personal-copilot"] + sourceCounts["personal-claude"],
      plugin_skill_count: sourceCounts.plugin,
      custom_skill_count: sourceCounts.custom,
      command_count: commandCount,
      user_invocable_count: userInvocableCount,
      model_invocation_disabled_count: modelInvocationDisabledCount,
      skill_error_count: loadResult.errors.length,
      skill_warning_count: loadResult.warnings.length,
    },
  };
}
</code></pre>

<h3>5.4 텔레메트리 프로퍼티 분류</h3>
<p>Copilot CLI는 텔레메트리 프로퍼티를 <strong>2개 계층</strong>으로 분류한다:</p>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>계층</th>
<th>필드명</th>
<th>설명</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><code>properties</code></td>
<td>공개</td>
<td>집계/대시보드용 (해시된 값)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>restrictedProperties</code></td>
<td>제한</td>
<td>내부 디버깅용 (원본 값)</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<p>이 이중 구조는 PII 보호와 디버깅 효율성을 동시에 달성한다.</p>
<hr>
<h2>6. 에이전트 프롬프트 시스템</h2>
<h3>6.1 프롬프트 구조</h3>
<p><strong>파일 경로:</strong> <code>/Users/jun/Developer/codex/151_copilot_cli/decoded/11_search/0647__slash-code-solution-user-example-reduce-tool-file-terminal-editing-resources.js</code></p>
<p>Copilot CLI는 XML 태그 기반의 구조화된 프롬프트 시스템을 사용한다:</p>
<pre><code>프롬프트 조립 순서:
1. &#x3C;tool_use_guidelines>       — 도구 사용 규칙 (rg 우선, 병렬 호출)
2. &#x3C;editing_constraints>       — 편집 규칙 (dirty worktree 존중, 비가역적 명령 금지)
3. &#x3C;exploration_and_reading_files> — 파일 탐색 규칙 (배치 읽기, 병렬화)
4. &#x3C;autonomy_and_persistence>  — 자율성 규칙 (end-to-end 완료 지향)
5. &#x3C;solution_persistence>      — 행동 편향 ("should we?" → 실행)
6. &#x3C;user_updates_spec>         — 사용자 업데이트 빈도/톤 (8 도구 호출마다)
7. &#x3C;reduce_aggressive_code_changes> — 설명 vs 변경 판별 규칙
8. &#x3C;tool_preferences>          — 셸 도구 대체 규칙 (grep, glob, view)
9. &#x3C;file_paths_for_edit_view_create> — 파일 경로 검증 규칙
10. &#x3C;preamble_messages>        — 진행 상황 공유 규칙
11. &#x3C;responses_to_users>       — 응답 형식 (GitHub Markdown)
</code></pre>
<h3>6.2 핵심 프롬프트 규칙</h3>
<p><strong>도구 우선순위:</strong></p>
<pre><code class="language-javascript">S$e = ({ shellConfig }) => {
  let shellName = shellConfig?.shellToolName ?? "bash";
  let isPowershell = shellName === "powershell";
  return `&#x3C;tool_preferences>
Use built-in tools instead of ${shellName} tools whenever possible.
* Use the **grep** tool instead of ${isPowershell ? "`Select-String`/`findstr`" : "`grep`/`rg`"}
* Use the **glob** tool instead of ${isPowershell ? "`Get-ChildItem`/`dir`" : "`find`/`ls`"}
* Use the **view** tool instead of ${isPowershell ? "`Get-Content`" : "`cat`/`head`/`tail`"}
&#x3C;/tool_preferences>`;
};
</code></pre>
<h3>6.3 report_progress 체크리스트</h3>
<pre><code class="language-javascript">// PR 자동화를 위한 진행 보고 규칙
const Y9t = `
* Use **report_progress** frequently to commit and push your changes to the PR.
* Use markdown checklists to track progress (- [x] completed, - [ ] pending)
* Keep the checklist structure consistent between updates
* Review the files committed by **report_progress** to ensure minimal scope
* Use .gitignore to exclude build artifacts (node_modules, dist)
`;
</code></pre>

<h3>7.2 네이티브 Prebuild 전략</h3>
<p>네이티브 모듈(<code>keytar.node</code>, <code>sharp</code> 등)은 <code>prebuilds/</code> 디렉토리에 플랫폼별 바이너리로 포함:</p>
<pre><code>prebuilds/
├── darwin-arm64/    # macOS Apple Silicon
├── darwin-x64/      # macOS Intel
├── linux-arm64/     # Linux ARM
├── linux-x64/       # Linux x64
└── win32-x64/       # Windows x64
</code></pre>

<h2>8. 모델 라우팅</h2>
<h3>8.1 Copilot API 프록시 구조</h3>
<p>Copilot CLI는 직접 LLM 프로바이더에 접속하지 않고, <strong>GitHub Copilot API</strong>를 프록싱 계층으로 사용한다. 이는 인증 토큰 교환 및 모델 접근 제어를 GitHub 인프라에서 처리하는 독특한 구조이다.</p>
<pre><code class="language-mermaid">flowchart LR
    A[Copilot CLI] --> B[api.githubcopilot.com]
    B --> C{모델 라우팅}
    C --> D[GPT-4o]
    C --> E[Claude Sonnet]
    C --> F[o3 / o4-mini]

    G["API 키 모드"] --> H[직접 Anthropic API]
    G --> I[직접 OpenAI API]
    G --> J[Azure OpenAI]
</code></pre>
<h3>8.2 SDK 모드 다중 프로바이더</h3>
<p><code>x_()</code> 함수가 true를 반환하면 SDK 모드가 활성화되어 Anthropic/OpenAI/Azure를 직접 사용할 수 있다.</p>
<hr>
<h2>9. 에러 처리 / 복구 패턴</h2>
<h3>9.1 도구 결과 실패 구조</h3>
<pre><code class="language-javascript">function Mhe(errorMessage) {
  return {
    textResultForLlm: errorMessage,     // LLM에 전달되는 에러 메시지
    resultType: "failure",               // 실패 구분자
    error: errorMessage,                 // 상세 에러
    sessionLog: errorMessage,            // 세션 로그
    toolTelemetry: {},                   // 빈 텔레메트리
  };
}
</code></pre>
<h3>9.2 Git 에러 방어</h3>
<pre><code class="language-javascript">// git 명령 실패 시 graceful degradation
try {
  let changes = await dYi(gitRoot);     // git diff HEAD
} catch (error) {
  if (error instanceof Error) {
    let msg = error.message.toLowerCase();
    if (msg.includes("not a git repository") ||
        msg.includes("fatal: bad revision") ||
        msg.includes("unknown revision or path not in the working tree") ||
        msg.includes("fatal: ambiguous argument 'head'"))
    {
      return await Omn(gitRoot);        // git status로 폴백
    }
  }
  throw error;
}
</code></pre>
<h3>9.3 maxBuffer 초과 처리</h3>
<pre><code class="language-javascript">// diff가 5MB를 초과하면 truncated 마킹
try {
  let { stdout } = await HM("git", ["diff", ref, "--no-color"], {
    maxBuffer: 5 * 1024 * 1024,
  });
} catch (error) {
  if (error.message.includes("maxBuffer"))
    return { diff: "", isTruncated: true };
  throw error;
}
</code></pre>
<hr>
<h2>10. IDE 통합</h2>
<h3>10.1 GITHUB_ASKPASS 통합</h3>
<pre><code class="language-javascript">// IDE가 GITHUB_ASKPASS를 설정하면 토큰을 동기적으로 교환
getGitHubAskpassToken() {
  let askpass = process.env.GITHUB_ASKPASS;
  if (askpass) {
    let result = spawnSync(askpass, [], {
      encoding: "utf8",
      timeout: 10000,
      maxBuffer: 1048576,
    });
    return result.stdout?.trim();
  }
}
</code></pre>
<p>이를 통해 VS Code, Codespace 등 IDE 환경에서 자동으로 인증 토큰을 교환한다.</p>
<h3>10.2 MCP 기반 도구 통합</h3>
<p>Copilot CLI는 <code>github-mcp-server</code>를 내장하여 GitHub API 도구를 제공:</p>
<ul>
<li><code>summarize_job_log_failures</code> — CI 실패 진단</li>
<li><code>get_job_logs</code> — CI 로그 조회</li>
<li><code>issue_read</code> — 이슈/코멘트 읽기</li>
</ul>

<h3>핵심 발견</h3>
<ol>
<li>
<p><strong>보안 우선 부트스트랩</strong>: <code>require</code>를 오버라이드하여 앱 디렉토리 밖의 모듈 로드를 물리적으로 차단하는 것은 다른 CLI 에이전트에서 찾아보기 어려운 방어 수준이다.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>7-타입 인증 체인</strong>: HMAC, SDK 토큰, API 키(Anthropic/OpenAI/Azure), Copilot API 토큰, GitHub PAT, keytar, gh CLI까지 — 가장 광범위한 인증 체계를 보유한다.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Classic PAT(ghp_) 명시적 차단</strong>: Fine-grained PAT, OAuth 토큰, GitHub App 토큰만 허용하며 Classic PAT를 보안상 거부한다.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>tree-sitter WASM 활용</strong>: Aider가 tree-sitter를 코드 맵(RepoMap)에 사용하는 것과 달리, Copilot CLI는 diff 하이라이팅(TUI 렌더링)에 특화하여 사용한다.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>GitHub Copilot API 프록시</strong>: 직접 LLM API가 아닌 Copilot API를 경유하는 구조로, 인증-라우팅-접근 제어가 GitHub 인프라에서 통합 관리된다.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>이중 텔레메트리 계층</strong>: <code>properties</code>(해시) + <code>restrictedProperties</code>(원문) 분리로 PII 보호와 디버깅을 동시에 달성한다.</p>
</li>
</ol>

<h2>Main analysis</h2>
<h1>Copilot CLI — Web Search Implementation</h1>
<blockquote>
<p><strong>Analysis snapshot</strong>: <code>151_copilot_cli/npm-package/</code> (<code>@github/copilot@1.0.7</code>) + <code>deobfuscated/</code> readable chunks. Copilot CLI is <strong>not</strong> a <code>codex-rs</code> checkout in this corpus — web search is implemented in the bundled TypeScript runtime.</p>
</blockquote>
<p>Copilot CLI exposes web research through <strong>three cooperating mechanisms</strong>: (1) OpenAI Responses <strong>hosted <code>web_search</code></strong> when the active model advertises <code>webSearch: true</code>, (2) <strong>GitHub MCP</strong> <code>github-mcp-server-web_search</code> aliased to <code>web_search</code> as fallback, and (3) a first-party <strong><code>web_fetch</code></strong> URL reader with markdown simplification.</p>
<h2>Architecture</h2>
<pre><code class="language-mermaid">flowchart TD
  Agent["Copilot agent session"] --> Route{"model.webSearch === true?"}
  Route -->|yes| Hosted["Responses API web_search"]
  Route -->|no| MCP["github-mcp-server-web_search → rename web_search"]
  Agent --> Fetch["web_fetch local tool"]
  MCP --> GH["GitHub MCP X-MCP-Toolsets: web_search"]
  Fetch --> Ler["Ler() fetch + turndown HTML→markdown"]
</code></pre>
<hr>
<h2>1. Dual-path <code>web_search</code></h2>
<h3>Tool list assembly (<code>C9t</code>)</h3>
<p>When building the MCP tool list for a session, Copilot <strong>removes</strong> the GitHub MCP search tool if the model already supports native hosted search:</p>
<pre><code class="language-javascript">// deobfuscated/.../slash-json-api-repos-copilot-embeddings-code-search-prompt-agent-examples.js:1146-1157
function C9t(t, items, n) {
  let r = items.findIndex((a) => a.name === R9t),
    o = r !== -1 ? items.splice(r, 1).at(0) : undefined,
    s = o ? { ...o, name: w9t, safeForTelemetry: true } : undefined;
  (n &#x26;&#x26; !v7e(n)) || (t?.webSearch !== true &#x26;&#x26; s &#x26;&#x26; items.push(s));
}
// R9t = "github-mcp-server-web_search", w9t = "web_search"
</code></pre>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Condition</th>
<th>Tool exposed</th>
<th>Backend</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><code>model.webSearch === true</code></td>
<td>Hosted <code>web_search</code> only</td>
<td>OpenAI Responses API server pipeline</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>model.webSearch !== true</code></td>
<td>Renamed MCP <code>web_search</code></td>
<td>GitHub MCP <code>web_search</code> toolset</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>v7e(settings) === false</code></td>
<td>Neither web path</td>
<td>Policy disables all web tools</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<p>Canonical minified mirror: <code>npm-package/app.js</code> (same <code>C9t</code> / <code>R9t</code> / <code>w9t</code> symbols).</p>
<h3>Policy gate (<code>v7e</code>)</h3>
<pre><code class="language-javascript">// deobfuscated/.../keyword-engine__0617.js:2206-2208
function v7e(t) {
  return !li(t, "copilot_swe_agent_disable_web_tools");
}
</code></pre>
<p>When <code>copilot_swe_agent_disable_web_tools</code> is active, <code>C9t</code> skips injecting MCP web search and <code>web_fetch</code> registration is also suppressed (<code>store/reindex.js:1734</code> uses <code>v7e(settings) ? msr(d) : undefined</code>).</p>
<hr>
<h2>2. GitHub MCP <code>web_search</code> integration</h2>
<h3>Toolset request (<code>client-registry.js</code>)</h3>
<p>Default GitHub MCP headers include <code>web_search</code> in allowed toolsets:</p>
<pre><code class="language-javascript">// deobfuscated/src/app/mcp/host/client-registry.js:21-31
let e = "repos,issues,users,pull_requests,code_security,secret_protection,actions,web_search";
// ...
"X-MCP-Toolsets": e,
"X-MCP-Host": "github-coding-agent",
</code></pre>
<p>Built-in MCP server lists <code>web_search</code> among GitHub tools (<code>keyword-engine__1223.js:66</code>).</p>
<h3>Rename rationale</h3>
<p>MCP tool arrives as <code>github-mcp-server-web_search</code> (namespaced). Copilot renames to <code>web_search</code> with <code>safeForTelemetry: true</code> so the agent sees a stable tool id matching hosted search and UI labels.</p>
<hr>
<h2>3. Hosted <code>web_search</code> (Responses API)</h2>
<p>When <code>t?.webSearch === true</code>, Copilot does <strong>not</strong> inject the MCP fallback. Search executes on the OpenAI Responses server — same pipeline as Codex (<code>121_openai-codex/codex-rs/</code>):</p>
<ul>
<li>Query decomposition → Bing + OAI-SearchBot → URL visit / passage extraction → rerank → citations</li>
</ul>
<p>Copilot-specific surface in this snapshot is the <strong>model capability flag</strong> (<code>webSearch</code>) and tool-list routing above — not a separate Rust <code>web_search.rs</code> in <code>151_copilot_cli/</code>.</p>
<p>For Rust <code>ToolSpec::WebSearch</code>, <code>WebSearchAction</code>, and <code>web_search</code> config modes (<code>live</code>/<code>cached</code>/<code>disabled</code>), see <code>014_web-search/14_co_web-search.md</code> (shared OpenAI stack lineage).</p>
<h3>Action types (Codex protocol reference)</h3>
<p>Hosted search uses the same action discriminant Codex documents:</p>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Action</th>
<th>Purpose</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><code>search</code></td>
<td>Keyword query (optional <code>queries</code> array)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>open_page</code></td>
<td>Navigate to URL</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>find</code> / <code>findInPage</code></td>
<td>Pattern search within page</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>other</code></td>
<td>Forward-compatible bucket</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<p>Copilot does not reimplement these in <code>151_copilot_cli/</code> — they are serialized by the Responses API and mapped in the shared <code>codex-rs</code> protocol layer when using Codex-class models.</p>
<hr>
<h2>4. <code>web_fetch</code> local tool</h2>
<h3>Registration (<code>keyword-engine__0617.js</code>)</h3>
<pre><code class="language-javascript">// :2252-2266
(C7e = "web_fetch"),
(BJt = class {
  getTool() {
    return {
      name: C7e,
      title: "Fetching web content",
      description: "Fetches a URL from the internet and returns the page as either markdown or raw HTML...",
      input_schema: Js(Isr),
      callback: this.webFetch.bind(this),
      safeForTelemetry: true,
    };
  },
</code></pre>
<h3>Parameters (<code>Isr</code> schema <code>:2231-2251</code>)</h3>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Param</th>
<th>Default</th>
<th>Max</th>
<th>Purpose</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><code>url</code></td>
<td>required</td>
<td>—</td>
<td>HTTP(S) target</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>max_length</code></td>
<td>5000 (<code>dsr</code>)</td>
<td>20000 (<code>usr</code>)</td>
<td>Response char cap</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>start_index</code></td>
<td>0</td>
<td>—</td>
<td>Pagination offset</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>raw</code></td>
<td>false</td>
<td>—</td>
<td>Raw HTML vs markdown</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<h3>Permission (<code>webFetch</code> <code>:2309-2321</code>)</h3>
<p>When <code>permissions.requestRequired</code>, calls <code>permissions.request({ kind: "url", intention: "Fetch web content", url })</code>.</p>
<h3>Protocol blocks</h3>
<ul>
<li><code>file:</code> rejected — directs agent to file read tool (<code>:2302-2308</code>)</li>
<li>Truncation note with next <code>start_index</code> (<code>:2339-2343</code>)</li>
</ul>
<h3>HTTP fetch core (<code>Ler</code>)</h3>
<pre><code class="language-javascript">// deobfuscated/src/app/core/slash-html-x-turndown-language--document-fragment.js:940-943
async function Ler(url, format, maxBytes, signal) {
  let o = AbortSignal.timeout(3e4),
    a = await fetch(url, { redirect: "follow", signal: s, headers: {} });
</code></pre>
<ul>
<li>30 s timeout (<code>3e4</code> ms)</li>
<li>Streaming read with <code>maxBytes</code> cutoff (<code>Fys = 10 * 1024 * 1024</code> in <code>keyword-engine__0617.js:2228</code>)</li>
<li>HTML → markdown via <code>ehs()</code> turndown path (<code>:981-990</code>)</li>
<li>On stream overflow appends <code>[Content truncated: exceeded N byte limit]</code> (<code>slash-html-...js:970-972</code>)</li>
</ul>
<h3>Success / failure telemetry (<code>webFetch</code> <code>:2347-2377</code>)</h3>
<p>Success returns <code>textResultForLlm</code> prefixed with <code>Contents of ${url}:</code> and metrics <code>originalContentLength</code> / <code>returnedContentLength</code>.</p>
<p>Failures capture <code>errorMessage</code> in <code>toolTelemetry.restrictedProperties</code>; URL hashed as <code>hashedUrl</code> for telemetry while full URL kept in restricted props (<code>:2279-2282</code>).</p>
<hr>
<h2>4b. Session tool assembly</h2>
<p><code>web_fetch</code> is registered only when web tools are enabled:</p>
<pre><code class="language-javascript">// deobfuscated/src/app/session/store/reindex.js:1734-1742
let f = v7e(settings) ? msr(d) : undefined,
  N = [u.shellTool, ...(u.otherTools ?? []), ...p, G, g, ...h, f, ...extraTools]
    .filter((tool) => tool !== undefined),
</code></pre>
<p><code>msr(d)</code> instantiates <code>BJt</code> (<code>keyword-engine__0617.js:2209-2211</code>). MCP <code>web_search</code> arrives separately via <code>C9t</code> during MCP tool merge — not in this array.</p>
<p>Order in combined tool list: shell → memory → editing → diagnostics → <strong><code>web_fetch</code></strong> → plugins/skills.</p>
<hr>

<h2>5. UI and agent protocol mapping</h2>

</code></pre>
<h3>Status strings (<code>keyword-engine__1526.js</code>)</h3>
<pre><code class="language-javascript">// :396-399 — web_search
let s = o &#x26;&#x26; o.length > 40 ? o.slice(0, 37) + "..." : o;
return s ? `Searching for '${s}'` : "Web search";

// :380-394 — web_fetch (hostname-aware truncation)
</code></pre>
<h3>ACP tool kind (<code>Pki</code> <code>:623-628</code>)</h3>

<th>Tool</th>
<th>Kind</th>
</tr>

<h3>Slash <code>/help</code> table (<code>slash-help-theme-model-compact-server.js:151-154</code>)</h3>
<p>Documents <code>web_fetch</code> params: <code>url</code>, <code>raw</code>, <code>max_length</code>, <code>start_index</code>. Notes: pagination via <code>start_index</code>; no auth headers.</p>
<h3>Allowed-tools frontmatter (<code>slash-tips-...js:290-293</code>)</h3>
<p><code>web_search</code>, <code>web_fetch</code>, <code>websearch</code>, <code>webfetch</code> map to <code>null</code> in <code>Har</code> — permission parser treats them as unrestricted category aliases.</p>

<h2>6. Comparison: Copilot CLI vs Codex vs OpenCode</h2>

</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>Primary search</td>
<td>Responses <code>web_search</code> OR GitHub MCP</td>
<td><code>ToolSpec::WebSearch</code> Rust</td>
<td>Exa/Parallel MCP <code>websearch</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Fallback</td>
<td>GitHub MCP rename</td>
<td>N/A</td>
<td>—</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>URL fetch</td>
<td><code>web_fetch</code> (JS)</td>
<td>—</td>
<td><code>webfetch</code> (Effect)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Disable switch</td>

</tr>

<hr>
<h2>7. Security notes</h2>
<h3><code>web_fetch</code></h3>
<ul>
<li>Blocks <code>file:</code> protocol (<code>keyword-engine__0617.js:2302</code>)</li>
<li>URL permission prompt when <code>requestRequired</code></li>
<li>10 MB byte stream cap (<code>Fys</code>), 20k char return cap (<code>usr</code>)</li>
<li>No custom auth headers in <code>Ler</code> fetch (<code>headers: {}</code>)</li>
</ul>
<h3>MCP <code>web_search</code></h3>
<ul>
<li>Requires <code>GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN</code> bearer (<code>client-registry.js:28</code>)</li>
<li>Tool exposure gated by <code>X-MCP-Toolsets</code> and optional <code>X-MCP-Exclude-Tools</code></li>
</ul>
<h3>Telemetry</h3>
<p>Renamed MCP tool sets <code>safeForTelemetry: true</code> on the <code>web_search</code> alias (<code>C9t</code>).</p>
<p><code>web_fetch</code> also sets <code>safeForTelemetry: true</code> on tool definition (<code>keyword-engine__0617.js:2265</code>). Telemetry uses hashed URL in properties while retaining restricted full URL for debugging (<code>:2279-2282</code>).</p>
<hr>
<h2>7b. Operational workflow (agent perspective)</h2>
<p>Typical multi-step web research in Copilot CLI:</p>
<ol>
<li><strong>Discovery</strong> — call <code>web_search</code> (hosted or MCP) with natural-language query; UI shows <code>Searching for '…'</code> (<code>keyword-engine__1526.js:396-399</code>).</li>
<li><strong>Deep read</strong> — call <code>web_fetch</code> on a result URL; markdown default simplifies HTML for token efficiency.</li>
<li><strong>Pagination</strong> — if response includes <code>&#x3C;note>Content truncated. Call the fetch tool with a start_index of N&#x3C;/note></code>, continue with <code>start_index=N</code> (<code>keyword-engine__0617.js:2339-2343</code>).</li>
<li><strong>Permission</strong> — first fetch to a domain may block on <code>permissions.request</code> until user approves (<code>:2309-2321</code>).</li>
</ol>
<p>This differs from OpenCode where <code>websearch</code> returns MCP blob text in one shot and <code>webfetch</code> is a separate Effect tool — Copilot's <code>web_fetch</code> pagination is more explicit in the tool contract.</p>
<hr>

<h2>Korean corpus (reference)</h2>
<blockquote>
<p>Verbatim KO corpus <code>014_web-search/14_cp_web-search.md</code> for cross-check; code blocks unchanged.</p>
</blockquote>
<h1>Copilot CLI — Web Search 구현</h1>
<blockquote>
<p><strong>분석 스냅샷</strong>: <code>151_copilot_cli/npm-package/</code> (<code>@github/copilot@1.0.7</code>) + <code>deobfuscated/</code> readable chunks. Copilot CLI is <strong>not</strong> a <code>codex-rs</code> checkout in this corpus — web search is implemented in the bundled TypeScript runtime.</p>
</blockquote>
<p>Copilot CLI exposes web research through <strong>three cooperating mechanisms</strong>: (1) OpenAI Responses <strong>hosted <code>web_search</code></strong> when the active model advertises <code>webSearch: true</code>, (2) <strong>GitHub MCP</strong> <code>github-mcp-server-web_search</code> aliased to <code>web_search</code> as fallback, and (3) a first-party <strong><code>web_fetch</code></strong> URL reader with markdown simplification.</p>
<h2>Architecture</h2>
<pre><code class="language-mermaid">flowchart TD
  Agent["Copilot agent session"] --> Route{"model.webSearch === true?"}
  Route -->|yes| Hosted["Responses API web_search"]
  Route -->|no| MCP["github-mcp-server-web_search → rename web_search"]
  Agent --> Fetch["web_fetch local tool"]
  MCP --> GH["GitHub MCP X-MCP-Toolsets: web_search"]
  Fetch --> Ler["Ler() fetch + turndown HTML→markdown"]
</code></pre>
<hr>
<h2>1. Dual-path <code>web_search</code></h2>
<h3>Tool list assembly (<code>C9t</code>)</h3>
<p>When building the MCP tool list for a session, Copilot <strong>removes</strong> the GitHub MCP search tool if the model already supports native hosted search:</p>
<pre><code class="language-javascript">// deobfuscated/.../slash-json-api-repos-copilot-embeddings-code-search-prompt-agent-examples.js:1146-1157
function C9t(t, items, n) {
  let r = items.findIndex((a) => a.name === R9t),
    o = r !== -1 ? items.splice(r, 1).at(0) : undefined,
    s = o ? { ...o, name: w9t, safeForTelemetry: true } : undefined;
  (n &#x26;&#x26; !v7e(n)) || (t?.webSearch !== true &#x26;&#x26; s &#x26;&#x26; items.push(s));
}
// R9t = "github-mcp-server-web_search", w9t = "web_search"
</code></pre>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Condition</th>
<th>Tool exposed</th>
<th>Backend</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><code>model.webSearch === true</code></td>
<td>Hosted <code>web_search</code> only</td>
<td>OpenAI Responses API server pipeline</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>model.webSearch !== true</code></td>
<td>Renamed MCP <code>web_search</code></td>
<td>GitHub MCP <code>web_search</code> toolset</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>v7e(settings) === false</code></td>
<td>Neither web path</td>
<td>Policy disables all web tools</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<p>Canonical minified mirror: <code>npm-package/app.js</code> (same <code>C9t</code> / <code>R9t</code> / <code>w9t</code> symbols).</p>
<h3>Policy gate (<code>v7e</code>)</h3>
<pre><code class="language-javascript">// deobfuscated/.../keyword-engine__0617.js:2206-2208
function v7e(t) {
  return !li(t, "copilot_swe_agent_disable_web_tools");
}
</code></pre>
<p>When <code>copilot_swe_agent_disable_web_tools</code> is active, <code>C9t</code> skips injecting MCP web search and <code>web_fetch</code> registration is also suppressed (<code>store/reindex.js:1734</code> uses <code>v7e(settings) ? msr(d) : undefined</code>).</p>
<hr>
<h2>2. GitHub MCP <code>web_search</code> integration</h2>
<h3>Toolset request (<code>client-registry.js</code>)</h3>
<p>Default GitHub MCP headers include <code>web_search</code> in allowed toolsets:</p>
<pre><code class="language-javascript">// deobfuscated/src/app/mcp/host/client-registry.js:21-31
let e = "repos,issues,users,pull_requests,code_security,secret_protection,actions,web_search";
// ...
"X-MCP-Toolsets": e,
"X-MCP-Host": "github-coding-agent",
</code></pre>
<p>Built-in MCP server lists <code>web_search</code> among GitHub tools (<code>keyword-engine__1223.js:66</code>).</p>
<h3>Rename rationale</h3>
<p>MCP tool arrives as <code>github-mcp-server-web_search</code> (namespaced). Copilot renames to <code>web_search</code> with <code>safeForTelemetry: true</code> so the agent sees a stable tool id matching hosted search and UI labels.</p>
<hr>
<h2>3. Hosted <code>web_search</code> (Responses API)</h2>
<p>When <code>t?.webSearch === true</code>, Copilot does <strong>not</strong> inject the MCP fallback. Search executes on the OpenAI Responses server — same pipeline as Codex (<code>121_openai-codex/codex-rs/</code>):</p>
<ul>
<li>Query decomposition → Bing + OAI-SearchBot → URL visit / passage extraction → rerank → citations</li>
</ul>
<p>Copilot-specific surface in this snapshot is the <strong>model capability flag</strong> (<code>webSearch</code>) and tool-list routing above — not a separate Rust <code>web_search.rs</code> in <code>151_copilot_cli/</code>.</p>
<p>For Rust <code>ToolSpec::WebSearch</code>, <code>WebSearchAction</code>, and <code>web_search</code> config modes (<code>live</code>/<code>cached</code>/<code>disabled</code>), see <code>014_web-search/14_co_web-search.md</code> (shared OpenAI stack lineage).</p>
<h3>Action types (Codex protocol reference)</h3>
<p>Hosted search uses the same action discriminant Codex documents:</p>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Action</th>
<th>Purpose</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><code>search</code></td>
<td>Keyword query (optional <code>queries</code> array)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>open_page</code></td>
<td>Navigate to URL</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>find</code> / <code>findInPage</code></td>
<td>Pattern search within page</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>other</code></td>
<td>Forward-compatible bucket</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<p>Copilot does not reimplement these in <code>151_copilot_cli/</code> — they are serialized by the Responses API and mapped in the shared <code>codex-rs</code> protocol layer when using Codex-class models.</p>
<hr>
<h2>4. <code>web_fetch</code> local tool</h2>
<h3>Registration (<code>keyword-engine__0617.js</code>)</h3>
<pre><code class="language-javascript">// :2252-2266
(C7e = "web_fetch"),
(BJt = class {
  getTool() {
    return {
      name: C7e,
      title: "Fetching web content",
      description: "Fetches a URL from the internet and returns the page as either markdown or raw HTML...",
      input_schema: Js(Isr),
      callback: this.webFetch.bind(this),
      safeForTelemetry: true,
    };
  },
</code></pre>
<h3>Parameters (<code>Isr</code> schema <code>:2231-2251</code>)</h3>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Param</th>
<th>Default</th>
<th>Max</th>
<th>Purpose</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><code>url</code></td>
<td>required</td>
<td>—</td>
<td>HTTP(S) target</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>max_length</code></td>
<td>5000 (<code>dsr</code>)</td>
<td>20000 (<code>usr</code>)</td>
<td>Response char cap</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>start_index</code></td>
<td>0</td>
<td>—</td>
<td>Pagination offset</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>raw</code></td>
<td>false</td>
<td>—</td>
<td>Raw HTML vs markdown</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<h3>Permission (<code>webFetch</code> <code>:2309-2321</code>)</h3>
<p>When <code>permissions.requestRequired</code>, calls <code>permissions.request({ kind: "url", intention: "Fetch web content", url })</code>.</p>
<h3>Protocol blocks</h3>
<ul>
<li><code>file:</code> rejected — directs agent to file read tool (<code>:2302-2308</code>)</li>
<li>Truncation note with next <code>start_index</code> (<code>:2339-2343</code>)</li>
</ul>
<h3>HTTP fetch core (<code>Ler</code>)</h3>
<pre><code class="language-javascript">// deobfuscated/src/app/core/slash-html-x-turndown-language--document-fragment.js:940-943
async function Ler(url, format, maxBytes, signal) {
  let o = AbortSignal.timeout(3e4),
    a = await fetch(url, { redirect: "follow", signal: s, headers: {} });
</code></pre>
<ul>
<li>30 s timeout (<code>3e4</code> ms)</li>
<li>Streaming read with <code>maxBytes</code> cutoff (<code>Fys = 10 * 1024 * 1024</code> in <code>keyword-engine__0617.js:2228</code>)</li>
<li>HTML → markdown via <code>ehs()</code> turndown path (<code>:981-990</code>)</li>
<li>On stream overflow appends <code>[Content truncated: exceeded N byte limit]</code> (<code>slash-html-...js:970-972</code>)</li>
</ul>
<h3>Success / failure telemetry (<code>webFetch</code> <code>:2347-2377</code>)</h3>
<p>Success returns <code>textResultForLlm</code> prefixed with <code>Contents of ${url}:</code> and metrics <code>originalContentLength</code> / <code>returnedContentLength</code>.</p>
<p>Failures capture <code>errorMessage</code> in <code>toolTelemetry.restrictedProperties</code>; URL hashed as <code>hashedUrl</code> for telemetry while full URL kept in restricted props (<code>:2279-2282</code>).</p>
<hr>
<h2>4b. Session tool assembly</h2>
<p><code>web_fetch</code> is registered only when web tools are enabled:</p>
<pre><code class="language-javascript">// deobfuscated/src/app/session/store/reindex.js:1734-1742
let f = v7e(settings) ? msr(d) : undefined,
  N = [u.shellTool, ...(u.otherTools ?? []), ...p, G, g, ...h, f, ...extraTools]
    .filter((tool) => tool !== undefined),
</code></pre>
<p><code>msr(d)</code> instantiates <code>BJt</code> (<code>keyword-engine__0617.js:2209-2211</code>). MCP <code>web_search</code> arrives separately via <code>C9t</code> during MCP tool merge — not in this array.</p>
<p>Order in combined tool list: shell → memory → editing → diagnostics → <strong><code>web_fetch</code></strong> → plugins/skills.</p>
<hr>
<h2>4c. Artifact verification (<code>PLAN.md</code>)</h2>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Fact</th>
<th>Evidence</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>Canonical runtime</td>
<td><code>151_copilot_cli/npm-package/app.js</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Version</td>
<td><code>@github/copilot@1.0.7</code> (<code>github-copilot-1.0.7.tgz</code>)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Public repo <code>v1.0.7</code> has <strong>no app source</strong></td>
<td>Reconstruction uses bundle deobfuscation, not <code>codex-rs</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Installed SHA match</td>
<td><code>PLAN.md:22</code> — npm <code>app.js</code> SHA equals live install</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<p>WP49 citations use <strong>deobfuscated</strong> paths mapped from <code>reconstructed_v2/modules/</code> chunk IDs (e.g. module <code>0646</code> = <code>C9t</code>, <code>1526</code> = ACP mapper).</p>
<hr>
<h2>5. UI and agent protocol mapping</h2>

<pre><code class="language-javascript">{ id: "web", label: "Web", tools: ["web_search", "web_fetch"] },
</code></pre>

return s ? `Searching for '${s}'` : "Web search";

// :380-394 — web_fetch (hostname-aware truncation)

<h3>Slash <code>/help</code> table (<code>slash-help-theme-model-compact-server.js:151-154</code>)</h3>

<p><code>web_search</code>, <code>web_fetch</code>, <code>websearch</code>, <code>webfetch</code> map to <code>null</code> in <code>Har</code> — permission parser treats them as unrestricted category aliases.</p>

<td>Primary search</td>

<hr>
<h2>7. Security notes</h2>
<h3><code>web_fetch</code></h3>
<ul>
<li>Blocks <code>file:</code> protocol (<code>keyword-engine__0617.js:2302</code>)</li>
<li>URL permission prompt when <code>requestRequired</code></li>
<li>10 MB byte stream cap (<code>Fys</code>), 20k char return cap (<code>usr</code>)</li>
<li>No custom auth headers in <code>Ler</code> fetch (<code>headers: {}</code>)</li>
</ul>
<h3>MCP <code>web_search</code></h3>
<ul>
<li>Requires <code>GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN</code> bearer (<code>client-registry.js:28</code>)</li>
<li>Tool exposure gated by <code>X-MCP-Toolsets</code> and optional <code>X-MCP-Exclude-Tools</code></li>
</ul>
<h3>Telemetry</h3>
<p>Renamed MCP tool sets <code>safeForTelemetry: true</code> on the <code>web_search</code> alias (<code>C9t</code>).</p>
<p><code>web_fetch</code> also sets <code>safeForTelemetry: true</code> on tool definition (<code>keyword-engine__0617.js:2265</code>). Telemetry uses hashed URL in properties while retaining restricted full URL for debugging (<code>:2279-2282</code>).</p>
<hr>
<h2>7b. Operational workflow (agent perspective)</h2>
<p>Typical multi-step web research in Copilot CLI:</p>
<ol>
<li><strong>Discovery</strong> — call <code>web_search</code> (hosted or MCP) with natural-language query; UI shows <code>Searching for '…'</code> (<code>keyword-engine__1526.js:396-399</code>).</li>
<li><strong>Deep read</strong> — call <code>web_fetch</code> on a result URL; markdown default simplifies HTML for token efficiency.</li>
<li><strong>Pagination</strong> — if response includes <code>&#x3C;note>Content truncated. Call the fetch tool with a start_index of N&#x3C;/note></code>, continue with <code>start_index=N</code> (<code>keyword-engine__0617.js:2339-2343</code>).</li>
<li><strong>Permission</strong> — first fetch to a domain may block on <code>permissions.request</code> until user approves (<code>:2309-2321</code>).</li>
</ol>
<p>This differs from OpenCode where <code>websearch</code> returns MCP blob text in one shot and <code>webfetch</code> is a separate Effect tool — Copilot's <code>web_fetch</code> pagination is more explicit in the tool contract.</p>
<hr>

50:T19109,<h2>Overview</h2>
<p><strong>Reverse engineering (015)</strong> — developer-depth section from the codex corpus. Structural highlights extracted from the analysis:</p>
<ul>
<li>이 축은 설치 help parity 문서의 다음 단계다. 기존 <code>00</code>~<code>14</code> 축은 주로 help/config/local-doc 표면을 비교했고, 이 축은 설치된 바이너리와 번들에서 복원 가능한 구조를 따로 기록한다.</li>
<li>| Level | 의미 | 사용할 수 있는 주장 |</li>
<li>| installed-help | <code>--help</code>, public command output, version output | 명령/플래그/공개 subcommand가 존재한다 |</li>
<li>| safe-local-doc | 설치 패키지의 docs, changelog, examples, manifest | 문서화된 schema/경로/기능 설명이 있다 |</li>
<li>| bundle-key | minified bundle에 남은 module key, source path, route name, proto name | 내부 모듈/라우터/프로토/컴포넌트가 존재한다 |</li>
<li>| binary-string | native binary <code>strings</code>, <code>file</code>, <code>otool</code>, <code>codesign</code>, <code>nm</code> | crate/package/protocol/tool/evaluator 이름이 embedded되어 있다 |</li>
<li>| runtime-trace | synthetic project에서 실제 명령을 실행한 결과 | 동작 semantics, precedence, permission enforcement를 확인했다 |</li>
<li>| private-artifact-name | 비공개 상태 파일의 경로/개수만 관찰 | artifact가 존재한다. 내용/schema 주장은 금지 |</li>
<li><code>/Users/jun/Developer/codex/015_reverse-engineering/33_source_deobfuscation_inventory.md</code> further separates source-like recovery levels. In this corpus, Cursor reaches preserved module-key recovery, while Grok/Kiro/AGY mostly expose native</li>
<li>auth/token/cookie/credential/login data</li>
<li>raw transcript, chat history, conversation DB/PB</li>
<li>logs and terminal captures</li>
</ul>

<h2>Main analysis</h2>
<h3><code>015_reverse-engineering/00_reverse_evidence_policy.md</code></h3>
<h1>Reverse Engineering Evidence Policy</h1>
<p>This axis is the next step in the installation help parity document. The existing <code>00</code> to <code>14</code> axis mainly compared the help/config/local-doc surface, and this axis separately records the structures that can be restored from installed binaries and bundles.</p>
<h2>Evidence Levels</h2>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Level</th>
<th>Meaning</th>
<th>Claims available</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>installed-help</td>
<td><code>--help</code>, public command output, version output</td>
<td>Command/Flag/Public subcommand exists</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>safe-local-doc</td>
<td>docs, changelog, examples, manifest in the installation package</td>
<td>There is a documented schema/path/function description</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>bundle-key</td>
<td>Module key, source path, route name, proto name remaining in minified bundle</td>
<td>Internal modules/router/prototypes/components exist</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>binary-string</td>
<td>native binary <code>strings</code>, <code>file</code>, <code>otool</code>, <code>codesign</code>, <code>nm</code></td>
<td>The name crate/package/protocol/tool/evaluator is embedded</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>runtime-trace</td>
<td>Results of executing actual commands in synthetic project</td>
<td>Checked operational semantics, precedence, and permission enforcement</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>private-artifact-name</td>
<td>Observe only the path/count of private state files</td>
<td>An artifact exists. Content/schema claims are prohibited</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<p><code>/Users/jun/Developer/codex/015_reverse-engineering/33_source_deobfuscation_inventory.md</code> further separates source-like recovery levels. In this corpus, Cursor reaches preserved module-key recovery, while Grok/Kiro/AGY mostly expose native source-path, handler, protobuf, and runtime-trace evidence. Do not call this "full source recovered" unless a real source map or readable source archive is present.</p>
<h2>Hard Boundary</h2>
<p>The purpose of reverse analysis is to restore product structure, not to extract user state. Next, reading the content is prohibited.</p>
<ul>
<li>auth/token/cookie/credential/login data</li>
<li>raw transcript, chat history, conversation DB/PB</li>
<li>logs and terminal captures</li>
<li>browser profile state, local storage, IndexedDB, session storage</li>
<li>memory/goal runtime stores</li>
<li>screenshots and browser recordings</li>
<li>private <code>mcp.json</code>, settings, config values unless explicitly redacted</li>
</ul>
<p>For private artifacts, only “exists” is used. Field/schema/content assertions are only used when there are public docs, source/bundle evidence, sanitized fixtures, and synthetic runtime traces.</p>

<h2>Reading Rule For Existing Docs</h2>
<p>When a <code>00</code>~<code>14</code> page says “None” or “Not supported”, interpret it as “Not exposed in installed help” unless this <code>15_reverse-engineering</code> axis confirms absence from bundle/binary evidence too.</p>
<p>When a page states runtime semantics from config/cache observation, this axis upgrades or downgrades the claim:</p>
<ul>
<li><code>bundle-key</code> or <code>binary-string</code> confirms a module exists.</li>
<li><code>runtime-trace</code> is still required for exact precedence, scheduling, isolation, merge, and permission behavior.</li>
</ul>

<li>Error mapping distinguishes disabled servers, team policy blocks, and team network allowlist blocks</li>
</ul>

<h3><code>015_reverse-engineering/20_synthetic_mcp_config_trace.md</code></h3>
<h1>Synthetic MCP Config Trace</h1>
<p>This pass used disposable temp HOME/workspace directories and dummy <code>/bin/echo</code> MCP commands. It did not start real MCP servers, use credentials, call model sessions, or read private user config.</p>
<h2>Cursor Agent</h2>
<p>Synthetic files:</p>
<ul>
<li>temp HOME: <code>&#x3C;tmp>/home/.cursor/mcp.json</code></li>
<li>temp workspace: <code>&#x3C;tmp>/ws/.cursor/mcp.json</code></li>
</ul>
<p>Config shape used:</p>
<pre><code class="language-json">{"mcpServers":{"homeDummy":{"command":"/bin/echo","args":["home"]}}}
</code></pre>
<p>Findings:</p>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Condition</th>
<th>Result</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>temp workspace <code>.cursor/mcp.json</code> only, non-git directory</td>
<td>not discovered</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>temp HOME <code>~/.cursor/mcp.json</code></td>
<td><code>homeDummy: not loaded (needs approval)</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>temp workspace after <code>git init</code></td>
<td><code>workspaceDummy: not loaded (needs approval)</code></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<p>Implications:</p>
<ul>
<li>Cursor Agent's synthetic trace supports <code>mcpServers</code> as the config root.</li>
<li>User-level <code>~/.cursor/mcp.json</code> is discovered with temp HOME.</li>
<li>Project <code>.cursor/mcp.json</code> discovery appears tied to workspace/git detection in this trace.</li>
<li>Servers are not auto-loaded; they require approval.</li>
</ul>
<p>Follow-up runtime trace:</p>
<p><code>/Users/jun/Developer/codex/015_reverse-engineering/29_cr_mcp_handshake_trace.md</code> used a project-scoped <code>.cursor/mcp.json</code> plus a synthetic stdio MCP server. <code>cursor-agent mcp enable trace</code> wrote an approval entry under temp HOME at <code>.cursor/projects/&#x3C;project-slug>/mcp-approvals.json</code>; the observed entry was <code>trace-0bab3cead8c72734</code>.</p>
<p><code>mcp list-tools trace</code> then crossed the pre-approval gate and launched the minimal hand-written server, but it timed out without sending any JSON-RPC <code>initialize</code> frame to that fixture.</p>
<p>A second fixture using <code>@modelcontextprotocol/server-everything</code> succeeded: <code>cursor-agent mcp list-tools everything</code> listed 14 tools. This confirms approval enforcement, project-state write behavior, and standalone no-account stdio MCP tool enumeration.</p>

<td>default agent bucket <code>kiro_default</code>, despite <code>--scope workspace</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Status scope</td>
<td><code>Scope: default</code>, <code>Agent: kiro_default</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Disabled flag</td>
<td><code>Disabled: true</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Default timeout</td>
<td><code>120000 ms</code></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<p>Implications:</p>
<ul>
<li>Kiro's CLI writes a workspace config file for the trace.</li>
<li>In this run, workspace-scoped server display/status resolved under the default agent bucket.</li>
<li>Disabled servers are listed and status-inspectable without launching the command.</li>
<li>A later enabled official-server status trace in <code>/Users/jun/Developer/codex/015_reverse-engineering/24_ki_agent_mcp_trace.md</code> confirmed JSON-array args for <code>@modelcontextprotocol/server-everything</code>, displayed <code>Disabled: false</code>, but still did not report protocol handshake or tool discovery.</li>
</ul>
<h2>AGY CLI</h2>
<p>AGY has no public <code>mcp</code> command group in the installed CLI help. No synthetic MCP config trace was run for AGY in this pass. Its MCP evidence remains binary/cache evidence plus public plugin import surface.</p>
<h2>Remaining Gaps</h2>
<ul>
<li>Cursor MCP project approval behavior and standalone no-account <code>tools/list</code> with an official stdio server are tested in <code>/Users/jun/Developer/codex/015_reverse-engineering/29_cr_mcp_handshake_trace.md</code>.</li>
<li>Grok native TOML MCP startup, handshake, and tool discovery are tested in <code>/Users/jun/Developer/codex/015_reverse-engineering/30_grok_mcp_doctor_trace.md</code>.</li>
<li>OAuth flows were not tested.</li>
<li>Kiro agent-specific MCP routing with a custom synthetic agent is tested in <code>/Users/jun/Developer/codex/015_reverse-engineering/24_ki_agent_mcp_trace.md</code>; actual server startup and protocol handshake remain separate gaps.</li>
<li>AGY MCP activation requires either hidden <code>agentapi</code> knowledge, plugin import, or model/session trace.</li>
</ul>

<h3><code>015_reverse-engineering/24_ki_agent_mcp_trace.md</code></h3>
<h1>Kiro Agent MCP Trace</h1>
<p>This pass used disposable temp <code>HOME</code> and workspace directories. It did not run model prompts, start real MCP servers, authenticate, or read private Kiro sessions.</p>
<h2>Public Help Surface</h2>
<p>Commands inspected:</p>
<ul>
<li><code>/Users/jun/.local/bin/kiro-cli-chat agent create --help</code></li>
<li><code>/Users/jun/.local/bin/kiro-cli-chat agent list --help</code></li>
<li><code>/Users/jun/.local/bin/kiro-cli-chat mcp add --help</code></li>
<li><code>/Users/jun/.local/bin/kiro-cli-chat mcp list --help</code></li>
</ul>
<p>Relevant help semantics:</p>
<ul>
<li><code>agent create [NAME] --directory &#x3C;DIRECTORY></code> creates an agent config in the chosen directory, but opens an editor by default.</li>
<li><code>agent list</code> says local agents are discovered only when invoked in a directory that contains them.</li>
<li><code>mcp add --agent &#x3C;AGENT></code> writes the server to the named agent.</li>
<li><code>mcp add --scope &#x3C;SCOPE></code> is only meaningful when no agent name is supplied.</li>
</ul>
<h2>Editor Behavior</h2>
<p><code>agent create trace --directory &#x3C;tmp>/ws/.kiro/agents</code> created <code>&#x3C;tmp>/ws/.kiro/agents/trace.json</code> and opened it in <code>vi</code>. The generated template shape was:</p>
<pre><code class="language-json">{
  "name": "trace",
  "description": "",
  "prompt": null,
  "mcpServers": {},
  "tools": ["*"],
  "toolAliases": {},
  "allowedTools": [],
  "resources": [],
  "hooks": {},
  "toolsSettings": {},
  "includeMcpJson": true,
  "model": null
}
</code></pre>
<p>The editor process was terminated after capturing the template. Follow-up traces used a directly written synthetic JSON file with the same shape to avoid interactive editor state.</p>
<h2>Workspace Agent Discovery</h2>
<p>Synthetic file:</p>
<ul>
<li><code>&#x3C;tmp>/ws/.kiro/agents/trace.json</code></li>
</ul>
<p>Commands:</p>
<ul>
<li><code>HOME=&#x3C;tmp>/home /Users/jun/.local/bin/kiro-cli-chat agent validate --path &#x3C;tmp>/ws/.kiro/agents/trace.json</code></li>
<li><code>HOME=&#x3C;tmp>/home /Users/jun/.local/bin/kiro-cli-chat agent list</code></li>
</ul>
<p>Findings:</p>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Surface</th>
<th>Result</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>validation</td>
<td>exit code 0, no output</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>agent list</td>
<td>shows built-ins plus <code>trace</code> as <code>Workspace</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>built-ins</td>
<td><code>kiro_default</code>, <code>kiro_help</code>, <code>kiro_planner</code> remain visible</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<h2>Agent-Specific MCP Routing</h2>
<p>Command:</p>
<ul>
<li><code>HOME=&#x3C;tmp>/home /Users/jun/.local/bin/kiro-cli-chat mcp add --name dummy --agent trace --command /bin/echo --args dummy --disabled --force</code></li>
</ul>
<p>Observed output:</p>
<ul>
<li><code>Added MCP server 'dummy' to agent trace</code></li>
</ul>
<p><code>mcp list</code> then grouped the server under workspace agent <code>trace</code>:</p>
<pre><code class="language-text">workspace:
  kiro_help
    (empty)
  kiro_planner
    (empty)
  trace
    - dummy        /bin/echo (disabled)
</code></pre>
<p><code>mcp status --name dummy</code> reported:</p>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Field</th>
<th>Result</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>Scope</td>
<td><code>workspace</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Agent</td>
<td><code>trace</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Command</td>
<td><code>/bin/echo</code></td>
</tr>

<td>Timeout</td>
<td><code>120000 ms</code></td>

<td>Disabled</td>
<td><code>true</code></td>

<p>The synthetic agent JSON was updated in place:</p>
<pre><code class="language-json">"mcpServers": {
  "dummy": {
    "command": "/bin/echo",
    "args": ["dummy"],
    "env": {},
    "disabled": true
  }
}
</code></pre>
<h2>Documentation Consequences</h2>
<ul>
<li>Kiro has a public no-model path for validating custom workspace agents and binding MCP servers to a specific agent.</li>
<li><code>--agent &#x3C;name></code> overrides <code>--scope</code>; agent-specific MCP config is stored inside the agent JSON rather than the workspace <code>.kiro/settings/mcp.json</code> path observed for non-agent workspace MCP.</li>
<li><code>includeMcpJson: true</code> is present in the generated agent template, but this trace does not prove its runtime inclusion semantics.</li>
</ul>
<h2>Official Server Status Follow-up</h2>
<p>A second temp workspace tested an enabled <code>@modelcontextprotocol/server-everything</code> config without a model session:</p>
<ul>
<li>temp HOME: <code>/tmp/kiro-mcp-official.anATMg/home</code></li>
<li>temp workspace: <code>/tmp/kiro-mcp-official.anATMg/ws</code></li>
<li>config file: <code>/tmp/kiro-mcp-official.anATMg/ws/.kiro/settings/mcp.json</code></li>
</ul>
<p>Command:</p>
<pre><code class="language-text">HOME=/tmp/kiro-mcp-official.anATMg/home /Users/jun/.local/bin/kiro-cli-chat mcp add --name everything --scope workspace --command /Users/jun/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.1/bin/npx --args '["-y","@modelcontextprotocol/server-everything"]' --env npm_config_cache=/tmp/kiro-mcp-official.anATMg/npm-cache --force
</code></pre>
<p>Result:</p>
<pre><code class="language-text">✓ Added MCP server 'everything' to workspace config in /private/tmp/kiro-mcp-official.anATMg/ws/.kiro/settings/mcp.json
</code></pre>
<p>The written config used:</p>
<pre><code class="language-json">{"mcpServers":{"everything":{"command":"/Users/jun/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.1/bin/npx","args":["-y","@modelcontextprotocol/server-everything"],"env":{"npm_config_cache":"/tmp/kiro-mcp-official.anATMg/npm-cache"}}}}
</code></pre>
<p><code>mcp status --name everything -vv</code> did not report protocol handshake or tool discovery. It reported:</p>
<ul>
<li>not logged in, no valid token found</li>
<li>workspace config path <code>/private/tmp/kiro-mcp-official.anATMg/ws/.kiro</code></li>
<li>global config path <code>/tmp/kiro-mcp-official.anATMg/home/.kiro</code></li>
<li>scope displayed as <code>default</code></li>
<li>agent displayed as <code>kiro_default</code></li>
<li>command path to <code>npx</code></li>
<li>timeout <code>120000 ms</code></li>
<li>disabled <code>false</code></li>
<li>env var <code>npm_config_cache=/tmp/kiro-mcp-official.anATMg/npm-cache</code></li>
</ul>
<h2>Remaining Gaps</h2>
<ul>
<li>Actual MCP server startup and protocol handshake.</li>
<li>Agent-specific MCP behavior during a model session.</li>
<li>Interaction between <code>includeMcpJson</code>, global/workspace MCP config, and agent-local <code>mcpServers</code>.</li>
<li>Custom agent execution, delegation, and tool-permission inheritance.</li>
</ul>

<h3><code>015_reverse-engineering/29_cr_mcp_handshake_trace.md</code></h3>
<h1>Cursor Agent MCP Handshake Trace</h1>
<blockquote>
<p><strong>June 2026 refresh:</strong> <code>42_cr_mcp_handshake_trace_jun2026.md</code> (<code>cursor-agent</code> <code>2026.06.26-7079533</code>, 14 tools, 2026-06-28). This file is the <strong>May 2026</strong> baseline pass.</p>
</blockquote>
<p>This pass used disposable temp HOMEs and temp git workspaces. It did not read private Cursor config, use credentials, call a model session, or mutate the user's real Cursor state.</p>
<h2>Minimal Fixture</h2>
<p>Synthetic workspace:</p>
<ul>
<li>temp workspace: <code>/tmp/cursor-mcp-final.DLswue/ws</code></li>
<li>temp HOME: <code>/tmp/cursor-mcp-final.DLswue/home</code></li>
<li>config file: <code>/tmp/cursor-mcp-final.DLswue/ws/.cursor/mcp.json</code></li>
<li>approval file written by Cursor Agent: <code>/tmp/cursor-mcp-final.DLswue/home/.cursor/projects/private-tmp-cursor-mcp-final-DLswue-ws/mcp-approvals.json</code></li>
</ul>
<p>The config shape was:</p>
<pre><code class="language-json">{"mcpServers":{"trace":{"command":"/Users/jun/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.1/bin/node","args":["/tmp/cursor-mcp-final.DLswue/trace-mcp.js"]}}}
</code></pre>
<p>The synthetic server was a no-network stdio process that logged startup, parsed <code>Content-Length</code> JSON-RPC frames, answered <code>initialize</code>, and returned one tool from <code>tools/list</code>.</p>
<h2>Approval Flow</h2>
<p>Command:</p>
<pre><code class="language-text">HOME=/tmp/cursor-mcp-final.DLswue/home /Users/jun/.local/bin/cursor-agent mcp enable trace
</code></pre>
<p>from the temp workspace returned:</p>
<pre><code class="language-text">✓ Enabled and approved MCP server: trace
</code></pre>
<p>Cursor Agent wrote:</p>
<pre><code class="language-json">[
  "trace-0bab3cead8c72734"
]
</code></pre>
<p>Implications:</p>
<ul>
<li><code>mcp enable</code> uses the current working directory, not only <code>--workspace</code>, to resolve project MCP config.</li>
<li>Project approval state is stored under temp HOME at <code>.cursor/projects/&#x3C;project-slug>/mcp-approvals.json</code>.</li>
<li>The approval key is derived from the server name, server config, and project root. The trace observed <code>trace-0bab3cead8c72734</code> for this fixture.</li>
</ul>
<h2>Minimal List Tools Attempt</h2>
<p>Command:</p>
<pre><code class="language-text">HOME=/tmp/cursor-mcp-final.DLswue/home /Users/jun/.local/bin/cursor-agent mcp list-tools trace
</code></pre>
<p>from the temp workspace returned after a longer timeout:</p>
<pre><code class="language-text">Failed to list tools: Failed to load MCP 'trace': MCP error -32001: Request timed out
Error: command failed unexpectedly.
</code></pre>
<p>The synthetic server log for the same run contained only:</p>
<pre><code class="language-text">START argv=["/Users/jun/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.1/bin/node","/tmp/cursor-mcp-final.DLswue/trace-mcp.js"]
STDIN_END
START argv=["/Users/jun/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.1/bin/node","/tmp/cursor-mcp-final.DLswue/trace-mcp.js"]
STDIN_END
</code></pre>
<p>No <code>initialize</code>, <code>notifications/initialized</code>, or <code>tools/list</code> frame reached the server before Cursor Agent closed stdin.</p>
<h2>Official MCP Server Success Trace</h2>
<p>To separate a fixture implementation issue from Cursor's MCP loader behavior, a second temp workspace used the official <code>@modelcontextprotocol/server-everything</code> package through <code>npx</code> with an isolated npm cache:</p>
<ul>
<li>temp workspace: <code>/tmp/cursor-mcp-official.onq9Rl/ws</code></li>
<li>temp HOME: <code>/tmp/cursor-mcp-official.onq9Rl/home</code></li>
<li>npm cache: <code>/tmp/cursor-mcp-official.onq9Rl/npm-cache</code></li>
<li>config file: <code>/tmp/cursor-mcp-official.onq9Rl/ws/.cursor/mcp.json</code></li>
</ul>
<p>Config shape:</p>
<pre><code class="language-json">{"mcpServers":{"everything":{"command":"/Users/jun/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.1/bin/npx","args":["-y","@modelcontextprotocol/server-everything"],"env":{"npm_config_cache":"/tmp/cursor-mcp-official.onq9Rl/npm-cache"}}}}
</code></pre>
<p>Approval command from the temp workspace returned:</p>
<pre><code class="language-text">✓ Enabled and approved MCP server: everything
</code></pre>
<p>Cursor wrote:</p>
<pre><code class="language-json">[
  "everything-154da186314e45d5"
]
</code></pre>
<p><code>cursor-agent mcp list-tools everything</code> succeeded and printed 14 tools:</p>
<pre><code class="language-text">Tools for everything (14):
- echo (message)
- get-annotated-message (messageType, includeImage)
- get-env ()
- get-resource-links (count)
- get-resource-reference (resourceType, resourceId)
- get-structured-content (location)
- get-sum (a, b)
- get-tiny-image ()
- gzip-file-as-resource (name, data, outputType)
- simulate-research-query (topic, ambiguous)
- toggle-simulated-logging ()
- toggle-subscriber-updates ()
- trigger-elicitation-request ()
- trigger-long-running-operation (duration, steps)
</code></pre>
<h2>Failed Variants</h2>
<p>Earlier variants are useful negative controls:</p>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Variant</th>
<th>Result</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>user-level <code>~/.cursor/mcp.json</code> only</td>
<td><code>trace: not loaded (needs approval)</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>project config plus <code>--workspace</code>, but command run outside workspace</td>
<td><code>mcp enable trace</code> did not find the server</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>project config from current workspace before approval</td>
<td><code>mcp list-tools trace</code> failed with <code>MCP server "trace" has not been approved</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>project config after approval with 20s timeout</td>
<td>server started, no protocol frame received, timeout killed the process</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>project config after approval with 65s timeout</td>
<td>server started twice, no protocol frame received, Cursor returned MCP timeout</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>official <code>@modelcontextprotocol/server-everything</code> after approval</td>
<td><code>mcp list-tools everything</code> succeeded and listed 14 tools</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<h2>Conclusions</h2>
<p>This trace upgrades Cursor MCP evidence from static bundle/config discovery to real approval-file behavior:</p>
<ul>
<li><code>mcpServers</code> is the operative project config root for <code>.cursor/mcp.json</code>.</li>
<li><code>mcp enable</code> creates project-scoped approval records in the Cursor project-state directory under HOME.</li>
<li><code>mcp list-tools</code> enforces approval before loading a project MCP.</li>
<li>The standalone <code>mcp list-tools</code> path can complete a no-account stdio MCP handshake when pointed at a known-good server package.</li>
<li>The minimal hand-written stdio fixture still timed out without receiving <code>initialize</code>, so it should not be used as evidence of Cursor protocol failure.</li>
</ul>
<p>The remaining gap is not basic <code>tools/list</code>; it is deeper runtime behavior inside an actual agent/model session: tool invocation policy, permission prompts, auth/OAuth servers, resource access, and how MCP tools appear in model-facing context.</p>
<hr>
<h3><code>015_reverse-engineering/30_grok_mcp_doctor_trace.md</code></h3>
<h1>Grok MCP Doctor Trace</h1>
<p>This pass used a disposable temp HOME and workspace. It did not read private Grok config, use credentials, call a model session, or mutate the user's real Grok state.</p>
<h2>Fixture</h2>
<ul>
<li>temp HOME: <code>/tmp/grok-mcp-doctor3.jsPmH7/home</code></li>

command = "/Users/jun/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.1/bin/npx"
args = ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-everything"]
env = { npm_config_cache = "/tmp/grok-mcp-doctor3.jsPmH7/npm-cache" }
enabled = true
</code></pre>

  "healthy_count": 1,

    {

      "healthy": true
    }
  ]
}
</code></pre>

<h2>CLI Add Negative Control</h2>
<p>Two attempts to configure this server through <code>grok mcp add</code> failed because <code>--args -y ...</code> was parsed as an unexpected CLI option:</p>
<pre><code class="language-text">error: unexpected argument '-y' found
tip: to pass '-y' as a value, use '-- -y'
</code></pre>
<p>Earlier simple traces confirmed <code>grok mcp add dummy --command /bin/echo --args dummy</code> writes to temp <code>~/.grok/config.toml</code>; this negative control is specific to leading-dash argument passing for <code>npx -y</code>.</p>
<h2>Conclusions</h2>
<ul>
<li>Grok's native TOML <code>mcp_servers.&#x3C;name></code> config can launch a real stdio MCP server in a no-account temp HOME.</li>
<li><code>grok mcp doctor --json</code> performs protocol-level startup validation, not just static config parsing.</li>
<li>The doctor path confirms protocol <code>2025-06-18</code> and tool discovery for a known-good MCP server.</li>
<li>Remote source discovery includes <code>grok.com</code>, but it is skipped without login in this trace.</li>
<li>Remaining MCP gaps are model-session tool invocation, OAuth/HTTP flows, project precedence layering, and plugin-provided MCP activation.</li>
</ul>
