<h3><code>015_reverse-engineering/00_reverse_evidence_policy.md</code></h3>
<h1>Reverse Engineering Evidence Policy</h1>
<p>이 축은 설치 help parity 문서의 다음 단계다. 기존 <code>00</code>~<code>14</code> 축은 주로 help/config/local-doc 표면을 비교했고, 이 축은 설치된 바이너리와 번들에서 복원 가능한 구조를 따로 기록한다.</p>
<h2>Evidence Levels</h2>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Level</th>
<th>의미</th>
<th>사용할 수 있는 주장</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>installed-help</td>
<td><code>--help</code>, public command output, version output</td>
<td>명령/플래그/공개 subcommand가 존재한다</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>safe-local-doc</td>
<td>설치 패키지의 docs, changelog, examples, manifest</td>
<td>문서화된 schema/경로/기능 설명이 있다</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>bundle-key</td>
<td>minified bundle에 남은 module key, source path, route name, proto name</td>
<td>내부 모듈/라우터/프로토/컴포넌트가 존재한다</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>binary-string</td>
<td>native binary <code>strings</code>, <code>file</code>, <code>otool</code>, <code>codesign</code>, <code>nm</code></td>
<td>crate/package/protocol/tool/evaluator 이름이 embedded되어 있다</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>runtime-trace</td>
<td>synthetic project에서 실제 명령을 실행한 결과</td>
<td>동작 semantics, precedence, permission enforcement를 확인했다</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>private-artifact-name</td>
<td>비공개 상태 파일의 경로/개수만 관찰</td>
<td>artifact가 존재한다. 내용/schema 주장은 금지</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<p><code>/Users/jun/Developer/codex/015_reverse-engineering/33_source_deobfuscation_inventory.md</code> further separates source-like recovery levels. In this corpus, Cursor reaches preserved module-key recovery, while Grok/Kiro/AGY mostly expose native source-path, handler, protobuf, and runtime-trace evidence. Do not call this "full source recovered" unless a real source map or readable source archive is present.</p>
<h2>Hard Boundary</h2>
<p>역분석의 목적은 제품 구조 복원이지 사용자 상태 추출이 아니다. 다음은 내용 읽기 금지다.</p>
<ul>
<li>auth/token/cookie/credential/login data</li>
<li>raw transcript, chat history, conversation DB/PB</li>
<li>logs and terminal captures</li>
<li>browser profile state, local storage, IndexedDB, session storage</li>
<li>memory/goal runtime stores</li>
<li>screenshots and browser recordings</li>
<li>private <code>mcp.json</code>, settings, config values unless explicitly redacted</li>
</ul>
<p>비공개 artifact는 “존재한다”까지만 쓴다. 필드/schema/content 주장은 public docs, source/bundle evidence, sanitized fixture, synthetic runtime trace가 있을 때만 쓴다.</p>
<h2>Installed CLI version pin (2026-06-27)</h2>
<p>Single source of truth for pinned <code>ay</code>/<code>cr</code>/<code>gr</code>/<code>ki</code> binaries:</p>
<ul>
<li><code>/Users/jun/Developer/codex/015_reverse-engineering/36_installed_cli_version_manifest.md</code></li>
</ul>
<p>WP5 trace shards (<code>38</code>–<code>41</code>) extend hubs without changing evidence-level definitions above.</p>

<h2>Reading Rule For Existing Docs</h2>
<p>When a <code>00</code>~<code>14</code> page says “없음” or “지원하지 않음”, interpret it as “installed help에서 노출되지 않음” unless this <code>15_reverse-engineering</code> axis confirms absence from bundle/binary evidence too.</p>
<p>When a page states runtime semantics from config/cache observation, this axis upgrades or downgrades the claim:</p>
<ul>
<li><code>bundle-key</code> or <code>binary-string</code> confirms a module exists.</li>
<li><code>runtime-trace</code> is still required for exact precedence, scheduling, isolation, merge, and permission behavior.</li>
</ul>

<h2>MCP And Approval Modules</h2>
<p>Recovered modules:</p>
<ul>
<li><code>./src/mcp/approval.tsx</code></li>
<li><code>./src/mcp/approve-plugin-mcps-on-install.ts</code></li>
<li><code>./src/mcp/disabled-store.ts</code></li>
<li><code>./src/mcp/error-utils.ts</code></li>
<li><code>./src/mcp/project-paths.ts</code></li>
<li><code>./src/mcp/scope.ts</code></li>
<li><code>./src/shared/lazy-mcp-lease.ts</code></li>
<li>UI modules for MCP tools/resources/servers under <code>./src/components/*mcp*</code></li>
</ul>
<p>Key behavior recovered from bundle snippets:</p>
<ul>
<li>User config path: <code>~/.cursor/mcp.json</code></li>
<li>Project config path: <code>&#x3C;projectRoot>/.cursor/mcp.json</code></li>
<li>State files: MCP approvals and disabled-store paths are derived through <code>project-paths.ts</code></li>
<li>Project MCP enable flow computes approval IDs for project servers and removes disabled entries</li>
<li>OAuth callback helper listens on localhost port <code>8787</code> and verifies callback <code>state</code></li>
<li>Error mapping distinguishes disabled servers, team policy blocks, and team network allowlist blocks</li>
</ul>
<p>Sanitized runtime trace in <code>/Users/jun/Developer/codex/015_reverse-engineering/29_cr_mcp_handshake_trace.md</code> confirms the approval path at runtime: project <code>mcp enable</code> writes <code>.cursor/projects/&#x3C;project-slug>/mcp-approvals.json</code> under HOME, <code>mcp list-tools</code> rejects unapproved project servers before loading them, and an official stdio MCP server can be loaded enough to enumerate 14 tools.</p>

<h2>Remaining Gaps</h2>
<ul>
<li>Exact subagent authoring schema and task scheduling.</li>
<li>MCP tool invocation inside a real agent/model session. Project approval JSON and standalone <code>tools/list</code> are now traced from sanitized fixtures.</li>
<li>Worker bridge auth and frame protocol.</li>
<li>Sandbox policy compilation details inside native <code>cursorsandbox</code>.</li>
<li>Runtime behavior of worktree setup with dirty files.</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<h3><code>015_reverse-engineering/20_synthetic_mcp_config_trace.md</code></h3>
<h1>Synthetic MCP Config Trace</h1>
<p>This pass used disposable temp HOME/workspace directories and dummy <code>/bin/echo</code> MCP commands. It did not start real MCP servers, use credentials, call model sessions, or read private user config.</p>
<h2>Cursor Agent</h2>
<p>Synthetic files:</p>
<ul>
<li>temp HOME: <code>&#x3C;tmp>/home/.cursor/mcp.json</code></li>
<li>temp workspace: <code>&#x3C;tmp>/ws/.cursor/mcp.json</code></li>
</ul>

</code></pre>

<p>Implications:</p>
<ul>
<li>Cursor Agent's synthetic trace supports <code>mcpServers</code> as the config root.</li>
<li>User-level <code>~/.cursor/mcp.json</code> is discovered with temp HOME.</li>
<li>Project <code>.cursor/mcp.json</code> discovery appears tied to workspace/git detection in this trace.</li>
<li>Servers are not auto-loaded; they require approval.</li>
</ul>
<p>Follow-up runtime trace:</p>
<p><code>/Users/jun/Developer/codex/015_reverse-engineering/29_cr_mcp_handshake_trace.md</code> used a project-scoped <code>.cursor/mcp.json</code> plus a synthetic stdio MCP server. <code>cursor-agent mcp enable trace</code> wrote an approval entry under temp HOME at <code>.cursor/projects/&#x3C;project-slug>/mcp-approvals.json</code>; the observed entry was <code>trace-0bab3cead8c72734</code>.</p>
<p><code>mcp list-tools trace</code> then crossed the pre-approval gate and launched the minimal hand-written server, but it timed out without sending any JSON-RPC <code>initialize</code> frame to that fixture.</p>
<p>A second fixture using <code>@modelcontextprotocol/server-everything</code> succeeded: <code>cursor-agent mcp list-tools everything</code> listed 14 tools. This confirms approval enforcement, project-state write behavior, and standalone no-account stdio MCP tool enumeration.</p>

<h3><code>015_reverse-engineering/24_ki_agent_mcp_trace.md</code></h3>
<h1>Kiro Agent MCP Trace</h1>
<p>This pass used disposable temp <code>HOME</code> and workspace directories. It did not run model prompts, start real MCP servers, authenticate, or read private Kiro sessions.</p>
<h2>Public Help Surface</h2>
<p>Commands inspected:</p>
<ul>
<li><code>/Users/jun/.local/bin/kiro-cli-chat agent create --help</code></li>
<li><code>/Users/jun/.local/bin/kiro-cli-chat agent list --help</code></li>
<li><code>/Users/jun/.local/bin/kiro-cli-chat mcp add --help</code></li>
<li><code>/Users/jun/.local/bin/kiro-cli-chat mcp list --help</code></li>
</ul>
<p>Relevant help semantics:</p>
<ul>
<li><code>agent create [NAME] --directory &#x3C;DIRECTORY></code> creates an agent config in the chosen directory, but opens an editor by default.</li>
<li><code>agent list</code> says local agents are discovered only when invoked in a directory that contains them.</li>
<li><code>mcp add --agent &#x3C;AGENT></code> writes the server to the named agent.</li>
<li><code>mcp add --scope &#x3C;SCOPE></code> is only meaningful when no agent name is supplied.</li>
</ul>
<h2>Editor Behavior</h2>
<p><code>agent create trace --directory &#x3C;tmp>/ws/.kiro/agents</code> created <code>&#x3C;tmp>/ws/.kiro/agents/trace.json</code> and opened it in <code>vi</code>. The generated template shape was:</p>
<pre><code class="language-json">{
  "name": "trace",
  "description": "",
  "prompt": null,
  "mcpServers": {},
  "tools": ["*"],
  "toolAliases": {},
  "allowedTools": [],
  "resources": [],
  "hooks": {},
  "toolsSettings": {},
  "includeMcpJson": true,
  "model": null
}
</code></pre>
<p>The editor process was terminated after capturing the template. Follow-up traces used a directly written synthetic JSON file with the same shape to avoid interactive editor state.</p>
<h2>Workspace Agent Discovery</h2>
<p>Synthetic file:</p>
<ul>
<li><code>&#x3C;tmp>/ws/.kiro/agents/trace.json</code></li>
</ul>
<p>Commands:</p>
<ul>
<li><code>HOME=&#x3C;tmp>/home /Users/jun/.local/bin/kiro-cli-chat agent validate --path &#x3C;tmp>/ws/.kiro/agents/trace.json</code></li>
<li><code>HOME=&#x3C;tmp>/home /Users/jun/.local/bin/kiro-cli-chat agent list</code></li>
</ul>
<p>Findings:</p>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Surface</th>
<th>Result</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>validation</td>
<td>exit code 0, no output</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>agent list</td>
<td>shows built-ins plus <code>trace</code> as <code>Workspace</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>built-ins</td>
<td><code>kiro_default</code>, <code>kiro_help</code>, <code>kiro_planner</code> remain visible</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<h2>Agent-Specific MCP Routing</h2>
<p>Command:</p>
<ul>
<li><code>HOME=&#x3C;tmp>/home /Users/jun/.local/bin/kiro-cli-chat mcp add --name dummy --agent trace --command /bin/echo --args dummy --disabled --force</code></li>
</ul>
<p>Observed output:</p>
<ul>
<li><code>Added MCP server 'dummy' to agent trace</code></li>

  kiro_help
    (empty)
  kiro_planner
    (empty)
  trace
    - dummy        /bin/echo (disabled)
</code></pre>

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Field</th>
<th>Result</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>Scope</td>
<td><code>workspace</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Agent</td>
<td><code>trace</code></td>
</tr>

<td>Command</td>
<td><code>/bin/echo</code></td>
</tr>

<td><code>120000 ms</code></td>

<p>The synthetic agent JSON was updated in place:</p>
<pre><code class="language-json">"mcpServers": {
  "dummy": {
    "command": "/bin/echo",
    "args": ["dummy"],
    "env": {},
    "disabled": true
  }
}
</code></pre>
<h2>Documentation Consequences</h2>
<ul>
<li>Kiro has a public no-model path for validating custom workspace agents and binding MCP servers to a specific agent.</li>
<li><code>--agent &#x3C;name></code> overrides <code>--scope</code>; agent-specific MCP config is stored inside the agent JSON rather than the workspace <code>.kiro/settings/mcp.json</code> path observed for non-agent workspace MCP.</li>
<li><code>includeMcpJson: true</code> is present in the generated agent template, but this trace does not prove its runtime inclusion semantics.</li>
</ul>
<h2>Official Server Status Follow-up</h2>

</code></pre>

</code></pre>
<p>The written config used:</p>
<pre><code class="language-json">{"mcpServers":{"everything":{"command":"/Users/jun/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.1/bin/npx","args":["-y","@modelcontextprotocol/server-everything"],"env":{"npm_config_cache":"/tmp/kiro-mcp-official.anATMg/npm-cache"}}}}

</ul>
<h2>Remaining Gaps</h2>

<h3><code>015_reverse-engineering/29_cr_mcp_handshake_trace.md</code></h3>
<h1>Cursor Agent MCP Handshake Trace</h1>
<blockquote>
<p><strong>June 2026 refresh:</strong> <code>42_cr_mcp_handshake_trace_jun2026.md</code> (<code>cursor-agent</code> <code>2026.06.26-7079533</code>, 14 tools, 2026-06-28). This file is the <strong>May 2026</strong> baseline pass.</p>
</blockquote>
<p>This pass used disposable temp HOMEs and temp git workspaces. It did not read private Cursor config, use credentials, call a model session, or mutate the user's real Cursor state.</p>
<h2>Minimal Fixture</h2>
<p>Synthetic workspace:</p>
<ul>
<li>temp workspace: <code>/tmp/cursor-mcp-final.DLswue/ws</code></li>
<li>temp HOME: <code>/tmp/cursor-mcp-final.DLswue/home</code></li>
<li>config file: <code>/tmp/cursor-mcp-final.DLswue/ws/.cursor/mcp.json</code></li>
<li>approval file written by Cursor Agent: <code>/tmp/cursor-mcp-final.DLswue/home/.cursor/projects/private-tmp-cursor-mcp-final-DLswue-ws/mcp-approvals.json</code></li>
</ul>
<p>The config shape was:</p>
<pre><code class="language-json">{"mcpServers":{"trace":{"command":"/Users/jun/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.1/bin/node","args":["/tmp/cursor-mcp-final.DLswue/trace-mcp.js"]}}}
</code></pre>
<p>The synthetic server was a no-network stdio process that logged startup, parsed <code>Content-Length</code> JSON-RPC frames, answered <code>initialize</code>, and returned one tool from <code>tools/list</code>.</p>
<h2>Approval Flow</h2>
<p>Command:</p>
<pre><code class="language-text">HOME=/tmp/cursor-mcp-final.DLswue/home /Users/jun/.local/bin/cursor-agent mcp enable trace
</code></pre>
<p>from the temp workspace returned:</p>
<pre><code class="language-text">✓ Enabled and approved MCP server: trace
</code></pre>
<p>Cursor Agent wrote:</p>
<pre><code class="language-json">[
  "trace-0bab3cead8c72734"
]
</code></pre>
<p>Implications:</p>
<ul>
<li><code>mcp enable</code> uses the current working directory, not only <code>--workspace</code>, to resolve project MCP config.</li>
<li>Project approval state is stored under temp HOME at <code>.cursor/projects/&#x3C;project-slug>/mcp-approvals.json</code>.</li>
<li>The approval key is derived from the server name, server config, and project root. The trace observed <code>trace-0bab3cead8c72734</code> for this fixture.</li>
</ul>
<h2>Minimal List Tools Attempt</h2>
<p>Command:</p>
<pre><code class="language-text">HOME=/tmp/cursor-mcp-final.DLswue/home /Users/jun/.local/bin/cursor-agent mcp list-tools trace
</code></pre>
<p>from the temp workspace returned after a longer timeout:</p>
<pre><code class="language-text">Failed to list tools: Failed to load MCP 'trace': MCP error -32001: Request timed out
Error: command failed unexpectedly.
</code></pre>
<p>The synthetic server log for the same run contained only:</p>
<pre><code class="language-text">START argv=["/Users/jun/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.1/bin/node","/tmp/cursor-mcp-final.DLswue/trace-mcp.js"]
STDIN_END
START argv=["/Users/jun/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.1/bin/node","/tmp/cursor-mcp-final.DLswue/trace-mcp.js"]
STDIN_END
</code></pre>
<p>No <code>initialize</code>, <code>notifications/initialized</code>, or <code>tools/list</code> frame reached the server before Cursor Agent closed stdin.</p>
<h2>Official MCP Server Success Trace</h2>
<p>To separate a fixture implementation issue from Cursor's MCP loader behavior, a second temp workspace used the official <code>@modelcontextprotocol/server-everything</code> package through <code>npx</code> with an isolated npm cache:</p>
<ul>
<li>temp workspace: <code>/tmp/cursor-mcp-official.onq9Rl/ws</code></li>
<li>temp HOME: <code>/tmp/cursor-mcp-official.onq9Rl/home</code></li>
<li>npm cache: <code>/tmp/cursor-mcp-official.onq9Rl/npm-cache</code></li>
<li>config file: <code>/tmp/cursor-mcp-official.onq9Rl/ws/.cursor/mcp.json</code></li>
</ul>
<p>Config shape:</p>
<pre><code class="language-json">{"mcpServers":{"everything":{"command":"/Users/jun/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.1/bin/npx","args":["-y","@modelcontextprotocol/server-everything"],"env":{"npm_config_cache":"/tmp/cursor-mcp-official.onq9Rl/npm-cache"}}}}
</code></pre>
<p>Approval command from the temp workspace returned:</p>
<pre><code class="language-text">✓ Enabled and approved MCP server: everything
</code></pre>
<p>Cursor wrote:</p>
<pre><code class="language-json">[
  "everything-154da186314e45d5"
]
</code></pre>
<p><code>cursor-agent mcp list-tools everything</code> succeeded and printed 14 tools:</p>
<pre><code class="language-text">Tools for everything (14):
- echo (message)
- get-annotated-message (messageType, includeImage)
- get-env ()
- get-resource-links (count)
- get-resource-reference (resourceType, resourceId)
- get-structured-content (location)
- get-sum (a, b)
- get-tiny-image ()
- gzip-file-as-resource (name, data, outputType)
- simulate-research-query (topic, ambiguous)
- toggle-simulated-logging ()
- toggle-subscriber-updates ()
- trigger-elicitation-request ()
- trigger-long-running-operation (duration, steps)
</code></pre>
<h2>Failed Variants</h2>
<p>Earlier variants are useful negative controls:</p>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Variant</th>
<th>Result</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>user-level <code>~/.cursor/mcp.json</code> only</td>
<td><code>trace: not loaded (needs approval)</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>project config plus <code>--workspace</code>, but command run outside workspace</td>
<td><code>mcp enable trace</code> did not find the server</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>project config from current workspace before approval</td>
<td><code>mcp list-tools trace</code> failed with <code>MCP server "trace" has not been approved</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>project config after approval with 20s timeout</td>
<td>server started, no protocol frame received, timeout killed the process</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>project config after approval with 65s timeout</td>
<td>server started twice, no protocol frame received, Cursor returned MCP timeout</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>official <code>@modelcontextprotocol/server-everything</code> after approval</td>
<td><code>mcp list-tools everything</code> succeeded and listed 14 tools</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<h2>Conclusions</h2>
<p>This trace upgrades Cursor MCP evidence from static bundle/config discovery to real approval-file behavior:</p>
<ul>
<li><code>mcpServers</code> is the operative project config root for <code>.cursor/mcp.json</code>.</li>
<li><code>mcp enable</code> creates project-scoped approval records in the Cursor project-state directory under HOME.</li>
<li><code>mcp list-tools</code> enforces approval before loading a project MCP.</li>
<li>The standalone <code>mcp list-tools</code> path can complete a no-account stdio MCP handshake when pointed at a known-good server package.</li>
<li>The minimal hand-written stdio fixture still timed out without receiving <code>initialize</code>, so it should not be used as evidence of Cursor protocol failure.</li>
</ul>
<p>The remaining gap is not basic <code>tools/list</code>; it is deeper runtime behavior inside an actual agent/model session: tool invocation policy, permission prompts, auth/OAuth servers, resource access, and how MCP tools appear in model-facing context.</p>
<hr>
<h3><code>015_reverse-engineering/30_grok_mcp_doctor_trace.md</code></h3>
<h1>Grok MCP Doctor Trace</h1>
<p>This pass used a disposable temp HOME and workspace. It did not read private Grok config, use credentials, call a model session, or mutate the user's real Grok state.</p>
<h2>Fixture</h2>
<ul>
<li>temp HOME: <code>/tmp/grok-mcp-doctor3.jsPmH7/home</code></li>
<li>temp workspace: <code>/tmp/grok-mcp-doctor3.jsPmH7/ws</code></li>
<li>isolated npm cache: <code>/tmp/grok-mcp-doctor3.jsPmH7/npm-cache</code></li>
<li>config file: <code>/tmp/grok-mcp-doctor3.jsPmH7/home/.grok/config.toml</code></li>
</ul>
<p>Config:</p>
<pre><code class="language-toml">[mcp_servers.everything]
command = "/Users/jun/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.1/bin/npx"
args = ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-everything"]
env = { npm_config_cache = "/tmp/grok-mcp-doctor3.jsPmH7/npm-cache" }
enabled = true
</code></pre>
<h2>Doctor Result</h2>
<p>Command:</p>
<pre><code class="language-text">HOME=/tmp/grok-mcp-doctor3.jsPmH7/home /Users/jun/.local/bin/grok mcp doctor everything --json
</code></pre>
<p>Result summary:</p>
<pre><code class="language-json">{
  "healthy_count": 1,
  "failing_count": 0,
  "servers": [
    {
      "name": "everything",
      "transport": "stdio",
      "source": "config",
      "healthy": true
    }
  ]
}
</code></pre>
<p>Checks reported by Grok:</p>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Check</th>
<th>Result</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>command found</td>
<td>passed, <code>/Users/jun/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.1/bin/npx</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>server started</td>
<td>passed, <code>0.0s</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>handshake OK</td>
<td>passed, protocol <code>2025-06-18</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>tools discovered</td>
<td>passed, <code>13 tools discovered</code></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<p>Source discovery in the same JSON:</p>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Source</th>
<th>Status</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><code>~/.grok/config.toml</code></td>
<td>found, <code>server_count: 1</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>~/.claude.json</code></td>
<td>not found</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>.mcp.json</code></td>
<td>not found</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>grok.com</code></td>
<td>skipped, reason <code>not logged in</code></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<h2>CLI Add Negative Control</h2>
<p>Two attempts to configure this server through <code>grok mcp add</code> failed because <code>--args -y ...</code> was parsed as an unexpected CLI option:</p>
<pre><code class="language-text">error: unexpected argument '-y' found
tip: to pass '-y' as a value, use '-- -y'
</code></pre>
<p>Earlier simple traces confirmed <code>grok mcp add dummy --command /bin/echo --args dummy</code> writes to temp <code>~/.grok/config.toml</code>; this negative control is specific to leading-dash argument passing for <code>npx -y</code>.</p>
<h2>Conclusions</h2>
<ul>
<li>Grok's native TOML <code>mcp_servers.&#x3C;name></code> config can launch a real stdio MCP server in a no-account temp HOME.</li>
<li><code>grok mcp doctor --json</code> performs protocol-level startup validation, not just static config parsing.</li>
<li>The doctor path confirms protocol <code>2025-06-18</code> and tool discovery for a known-good MCP server.</li>
<li>Remote source discovery includes <code>grok.com</code>, but it is skipped without login in this trace.</li>
<li>Remaining MCP gaps are model-session tool invocation, OAuth/HTTP flows, project precedence layering, and plugin-provided MCP activation.</li>
</ul>

<h2>000/00_grand_synthesis.md (excerpt)</h2>
<h1>GRAND SYNTHESIS — 5 coding agents × 13 dimensions final comprehensive analysis</h1>
<p>This document is a capstone document that simultaneously compares Codex, Aider, Gemini CLI, OpenCode, and Claude Code at 13 architecture levels.
Rather than simply rearranging the conclusions of individual comparison documents, the goal is to read what product strategies different design philosophies converge into.</p>
<pre><code class="language-mermaid">graph TD
    A[5개 에이전트 개요] --> B[13개 차원 비교]
    B --> C[공통 패턴 추출]
    B --> D[핵심 차별점 추출]
    C --> E[기술 선택 시사점]
    D --> E
    E --> F[미래 전망과 설계 교훈]
</code></pre>
<h2>1. Overview</h2>
<h3>Purpose of this document</h3>
<p>The purpose of this document is to create a high-level reference that provides a view of five agents at once.
Although individual documents explain the superiority or inferiority of certain axes, actual product selection always requires simultaneous judgment on multiple axes.
Therefore, here we regroup TUI, prompt, tool, skill, subagent, app-server, sandbox, config, MCP, memory, plugin, guardian, and any remaining cross-cutting concerns into one frame.</p>
<ul>
<li>Basis: [[00_co_overview]], [[00_ai_overview]], [[00_ge_overview]], [[00_oc_overview]], [[00_cc_overview]]</li>
</ul>
<h3>Scope of analysis</h3>
<p>The scope is 5 agents and 13 dimensions.
The agents are Codex, Aider, Gemini CLI, OpenCode, and Claude Code.
The dimensions are TUI, Prompt/Context, Tool Runtime, Skill, Subagents, App-Server Surface, Sandbox, Config, MCP, Memory, Plugin, Guardian, and remaining cross-cutting concerns (Git/VCS, model routing, error handling, telemetry, IDE integration, packaging, testing, authentication, multimodal, event system, feature flags, internationalization).
As for the App-Server axis, the comparison document itself is written with a focus on Codex, Gemini CLI, and OpenCode, so Aider and Claude Code interpret even “intentional absence” together.</p>
<ul>
<li>Basis: [[001_tui-design/00_tui]], [[002_prompt-context/00_prompt]], [[003_tool-runtime/00_tool]], [[004_skill-system/00_skill]], [[005_subagents-collab/00_subagent]], [[006_app-server-client-surfaces/00_surface]], [[007_sandbox-exec-policy/00_sandbox]], [[008_config-system/00_config]], [[009_mcp-orchestration/00_mcp]], [[010_memory-pipeline/00_memory]], [[011_plugin-system/00_plugin]], [[012_guardian-approval/00_guardian]], [[013_etc/00_etc]]</li>
</ul>
<h3>Analysis methodology</h3>
<p>First, the technology stack, package structure, and core philosophy were extracted from each agent overview document.
Second, we collected axis-specific advantages and deficits, scouting scores, and design patterns from 13 comparison documents.
Third, patterns repeatedly seen by the same agent on multiple axes were grouped together and reinterpreted as a long-term strategy.
Fourth, rather than finding a single winner, practical recommendations were drawn based on “which design is more persuasive in which situation.”</p>
<ul>
<li>Evidence: [[00_co_overview]], [[00_ai_overview]], [[00_ge_overview]], [[00_oc_overview]], [[00_cc_overview]], [[001_tui-design/00_tui]], [[012_guardian-approval/00_guardian]]</li>
</ul>
<h3>How to read this document</h3>
<p>If your goal is to select a product, it is best to read Chapters 3, 7, and 8 first.
If your goal is to fork or reimplement, it is best to read Chapters 2, 5, 6, and 9 first.
If your purpose is a security review, it is better to read Chapters 3, 4, 8, and 9 together.
The last appendix serves as a document index, so you can use it to return to the detailed original text.</p>
<ul>
<li>Evidence: [[007_sandbox-exec-policy/00_sandbox]], [[009_mcp-orchestration/00_mcp]], [[011_plugin-system/00_plugin]], [[012_guardian-approval/00_guardian]]</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<h2>2. Agent Profile Summary</h2>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>agent</th>
<th>Technology Stack</th>
<th>Design Philosophy</th>
<th>Key Differentiators</th>
<th>Suitability for practice</th>
<th>Supporting Document</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>Codex</td>
<td>Rust + TypeScript + React/Ink + app-server protocol</td>
<td>The engine is separated into layers, and prompt, tool, skill, subagent, and MCP are treated as control planes.</td>
<td>The most decomposable core engine, the strongest terminal-native orchestration, and the most balanced sandbox/MCP combination.</td>
<td>A team where security, orchestration, and a forkable core engine are important</td>
<td>[[00_co_overview]], [[001_tui-design/00_tui]], [[002_prompt-context/00_prompt]]</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Aider</td>
<td>Python + prompt_toolkit + rich + tree-sitter + Git</td>
<td>Focus on the code editing loop rather than the tool platform. Optimize the model to make it a good editor.</td>
<td>Experience with RepoMap, edit strategy diversity, git auto-commit, and minimal friction CLI.</td>
<td>Use cases where individual developers, fast code editing, and low learning costs are important</td>
<td>[[00_ai_overview]], [[001_tui-design/00_tui]], [[002_prompt-context/00_prompt]]</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Gemini CLI</td>
<td>TypeScript + React/Ink + Zod + A2A server + multi-package monorepo</td>
<td>By combining schema-first and policy-first, CLI is treated like an extended operating system.</td>
<td>The most operational TypeScript engine with separate PromptProvider/Scheduler/PolicyEngine/ModelRouter/ExtensionManager.</td>
<td>Enterprise CLI, extended operations, policy auditing, TypeScript-based reimplementation</td>
<td>[[00_ge_overview]], [[001_tui-design/00_tui]], [[004_skill-system/00_skill]]</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>OpenCode</td>
<td>TypeScript + Bun + Effect-TS + Hono + SolidJS + Tauri</td>
<td>The agent runtime is viewed as a product platform shared by web/desktop/SDK.</td>
<td>Effect-TS service layer, 21+ provider, plugin SDK, session replay, multi-surface delivery.</td>
<td>Teams that want to quickly commercialize, include GUI/desktop, and expand directly to TS</td>
<td>[[00_oc_overview]], [[001_tui-design/00_tui]], [[003_tool-runtime/00_tool]]</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Claude Code</td>
<td>TypeScript/JavaScript bundle + React/Ink + custom renderer + Anthropic runtime</td>
<td>We prioritize product usability and deeply tie together hooks, security chains, memory, and MCP.</td>
<td>Richest productized tool surface, deepest shell security, strongest file-based memory UX.</td>
<td>Commercial CLI UX, strong shell security, memory-heavy workflows, enterprise policy integration</td>
<td>[[00_cc_overview]], [[001_tui-design/00_tui]], [[005_subagents-collab/00_subagent]]</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<h3>Codex</h3>
<ul>
<li>Identity: Separate the engine into layers, and treat prompt·tool·skill·subagent·MCP as the control plane.</li>
<li>Technology stack: Rust + TypeScript + React/Ink + app-server protocol</li>
<li>Key differentiation: most decomposable core engine, strongest terminal-native orchestration, most balanced sandbox/MCP combination.</li>
<li>Strong situation in practice: Teams where security, orchestration, and a forkable core engine are important</li>
<li>One-line review from a re-implementation perspective: A structure that separates the requirements constraint channel and prompt fragment provenance.</li>
<li>Basis: [[00_co_overview]], [[001_tui-design/00_tui]], [[002_prompt-context/00_prompt]], [[003_tool-runtime/00_tool]], [[005_subagents-collab/00_subagent]], [[007_sandbox-exec-policy/00_sandbox]], [[009_mcp-orchestration/00_mcp]]</li>
</ul>
<h3>Aider</h3>
<ul>
<li>Identity: Focus on the code editing loop rather than the tool platform. Optimize the model to make it a good editor.</li>
<li>Technology stack: Python + prompt_toolkit + rich + tree-sitter + Git</li>
<li>Key differentiators: RepoMap, edit strategy diversity, git auto-com</li>
</ul>

<h2>변경 기록</h2>
<h2>변경 기록</h2>
<ul>
<li>2026-03-26: Copilot CLI 역공학 문서를 완전한 역공학 설명 형식으로 대폭 확장. 번들 분석 개요(520K줄 디컴파일 파이프라인), 3단 부트스트랩, APCA 색상 과학, BasicTruncator 3단계 + CompactionProcessor 비동기 압축, ACP 30+ Zod 프로토콜, 7종 Permission Kinds, 5개 YAML 에이전트 정의(실제 yaml 증거 포함), promptParts 5토글, 원격 위임 5단계 상태 머신, MCP-ACP 브릿지, nano-AIU 과금, 3사 비교 분석표 통합.</li>
</ul>
<h2>000/00_grand_synthesis.md (발췌)</h2>
<h1>GRAND SYNTHESIS — 5개 코딩 에이전트 × 13개 차원 최종 종합 분석</h1>
<p>이 문서는 Codex, Aider, Gemini CLI, OpenCode, Claude Code를 13개 아키텍처 차원에서 동시에 비교한 캡스톤 문서다.
개별 비교 문서의 결론을 단순 재배열하지 않고, 서로 다른 설계 철학이 어떤 제품 전략으로 수렴하는지까지 읽어내는 것을 목표로 한다.</p>
<pre><code class="language-mermaid">graph TD
    A[5개 에이전트 개요] --> B[13개 차원 비교]
    B --> C[공통 패턴 추출]
    B --> D[핵심 차별점 추출]
    C --> E[기술 선택 시사점]
    D --> E
    E --> F[미래 전망과 설계 교훈]
</code></pre>
<h2>1. 개요</h2>
<h3>이 문서의 목적</h3>
<p>이 문서의 목적은 5개 에이전트를 한 번에 조망할 수 있는 상위 레퍼런스를 만드는 것이다.
개별 문서는 특정 축의 우열을 설명하지만, 실제 제품 선택은 언제나 여러 축의 동시 판단을 요구한다.
따라서 여기서는 TUI, prompt, tool, skill, subagent, app-server, sandbox, config, MCP, memory, plugin, guardian, 그리고 잔여 교차 관심사를 하나의 프레임으로 다시 묶는다.</p>
<ul>
<li>근거: [[00_co_overview]], [[00_ai_overview]], [[00_ge_overview]], [[00_oc_overview]], [[00_cc_overview]]</li>
</ul>
<h3>분석 범위</h3>
<p>범위는 5개 에이전트와 13개 차원이다.
에이전트는 Codex, Aider, Gemini CLI, OpenCode, Claude Code다.
차원은 TUI, Prompt/Context, Tool Runtime, Skill, Subagents, App-Server Surface, Sandbox, Config, MCP, Memory, Plugin, Guardian, 잔여 교차 관심사(Git/VCS, 모델 라우팅, 에러 처리, 텔레메트리, IDE 통합, 패키징, 테스트, 인증, 멀티모달, 이벤트 시스템, 피처 플래그, 국제화)다.
App-Server 축은 비교 문서 자체가 Codex·Gemini CLI·OpenCode 중심으로 작성되어 있으므로 Aider와 Claude Code는 “의도적 부재”까지 함께 해석한다.</p>
<ul>
<li>근거: [[001_tui-design/00_tui]], [[002_prompt-context/00_prompt]], [[003_tool-runtime/00_tool]], [[004_skill-system/00_skill]], [[005_subagents-collab/00_subagent]], [[006_app-server-client-surfaces/00_surface]], [[007_sandbox-exec-policy/00_sandbox]], [[008_config-system/00_config]], [[009_mcp-orchestration/00_mcp]], [[010_memory-pipeline/00_memory]], [[011_plugin-system/00_plugin]], [[012_guardian-approval/00_guardian]], [[013_etc/00_etc]]</li>
</ul>
<h3>분석 방법론</h3>
<p>첫째, 각 에이전트 개요 문서에서 기술 스택, 패키지 구조, 핵심 철학을 추출했다.
둘째, 13개 비교 문서에서 축별 우위와 결손, 스카우팅 점수, 디자인 패턴을 수집했다.
셋째, 같은 에이전트가 여러 축에서 반복적으로 보이는 패턴을 묶어 장기 전략으로 재해석했다.
넷째, 단일 승자를 찾기보다 “어떤 상황에서 어떤 설계가 더 설득력 있는가”를 기준으로 실무 추천안을 도출했다.</p>
<ul>
<li>근거: [[00_co_overview]], [[00_ai_overview]], [[00_ge_overview]], [[00_oc_overview]], [[00_cc_overview]], [[001_tui-design/00_tui]], [[012_guardian-approval/00_guardian]]</li>
</ul>
<h3>이 문서를 읽는 방법</h3>
<p>제품 선택이 목적이면 3장, 7장, 8장을 먼저 보는 것이 좋다.
포크나 재구현이 목적이면 2장, 5장, 6장, 9장을 먼저 보는 것이 좋다.
보안 검토가 목적이면 3장, 4장, 8장, 9장을 함께 읽는 편이 낫다.
마지막 부록은 문서 인덱스 역할을 하므로 세부 원문으로 돌아갈 때 사용하면 된다.</p>
<ul>
<li>근거: [[007_sandbox-exec-policy/00_sandbox]], [[009_mcp-orchestration/00_mcp]], [[011_plugin-system/00_plugin]], [[012_guardian-approval/00_guardian]]</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<h2>2. 에이전트 프로필 요약</h2>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>에이전트</th>
<th>기술 스택</th>
<th>설계 철학</th>
<th>핵심 차별점</th>
<th>실무 적합도</th>
<th>근거 문서</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>Codex</td>
<td>Rust + TypeScript + React/Ink + app-server protocol</td>
<td>엔진을 층으로 분리하고, prompt·tool·skill·subagent·MCP를 제어면(control plane)으로 다룬다.</td>
<td>가장 분해 가능한 core engine, 가장 강한 terminal-native orchestration, 가장 균형 잡힌 sandbox/MCP 결합.</td>
<td>보안, 오케스트레이션, 포크 가능한 core engine이 중요한 팀</td>
<td>[[00_co_overview]], [[001_tui-design/00_tui]], [[002_prompt-context/00_prompt]]</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Aider</td>
<td>Python + prompt_toolkit + rich + tree-sitter + Git</td>
<td>도구 플랫폼보다 코드 편집 루프에 집중한다. 모델을 좋은 editor로 만드는 쪽에 최적화한다.</td>
<td>RepoMap, edit 전략 다양성, git auto-commit, 최소 마찰 CLI 경험.</td>
<td>개인 개발자, 빠른 코드 편집, 낮은 학습 비용이 중요한 사용 사례</td>
<td>[[00_ai_overview]], [[001_tui-design/00_tui]], [[002_prompt-context/00_prompt]]</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Gemini CLI</td>
<td>TypeScript + React/Ink + Zod + A2A server + multi-package monorepo</td>
<td>schema-first와 policy-first를 결합해 CLI를 확장 운영체계처럼 다룬다.</td>
<td>PromptProvider/Scheduler/PolicyEngine/ModelRouter/ExtensionManager가 분리된 가장 운영형 TypeScript engine.</td>
<td>엔터프라이즈 CLI, 확장 운영, 정책 감사, TypeScript 기반 재구현</td>
<td>[[00_ge_overview]], [[001_tui-design/00_tui]], [[004_skill-system/00_skill]]</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>OpenCode</td>
<td>TypeScript + Bun + Effect-TS + Hono + SolidJS + Tauri</td>
<td>agent runtime을 웹/데스크톱/SDK가 공유하는 product platform으로 본다.</td>
<td>Effect-TS service layer, 21+ provider, plugin SDK, session replay, multi-surface delivery.</td>
<td>빠른 제품화, GUI/desktop 포함, TS로 직접 확장하고 싶은 팀</td>
<td>[[00_oc_overview]], [[001_tui-design/00_tui]], [[003_tool-runtime/00_tool]]</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Claude Code</td>
<td>TypeScript/JavaScript bundle + React/Ink + custom renderer + Anthropic runtime</td>
<td>제품 사용성을 최우선으로 하면서 hook, security chain, memory, MCP를 깊게 묶는다.</td>
<td>가장 풍부한 productized tool surface, deepest shell security, strongest file-based memory UX.</td>
<td>상용 CLI UX, 강한 shell security, memory-heavy workflows, enterprise policy integration</td>
<td>[[00_cc_overview]], [[001_tui-design/00_tui]], [[005_subagents-collab/00_subagent]]</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<h3>Codex</h3>
<ul>
<li>정체성: 엔진을 층으로 분리하고, prompt·tool·skill·subagent·MCP를 제어면(control plane)으로 다룬다.</li>
<li>기술 스택: Rust + TypeScript + React/Ink + app-server protocol</li>
<li>핵심 차별점: 가장 분해 가능한 core engine, 가장 강한 terminal-native orchestration, 가장 균형 잡힌 sandbox/MCP 결합.</li>
<li>실무에서 강한 상황: 보안, 오케스트레이션, 포크 가능한 core engine이 중요한 팀</li>
<li>재구현 관점 한 줄 평: requirements 제약 채널과 prompt fragment provenance를 분리한 구조.</li>
<li>근거: [[00_co_overview]], [[001_tui-design/00_tui]], [[002_prompt-context/00_prompt]], [[003_tool-runtime/00_tool]], [[005_subagents-collab/00_subagent]], [[007_sandbox-exec-policy/00_sandbox]], [[009_mcp-orchestration/00_mcp]]</li>
</ul>
<h3>Aider</h3>
<ul>
<li>정체성: 도구 플랫폼보다 코드 편집 루프에 집중한다. 모델을 좋은 editor로 만드는 쪽에 최적화한다.</li>
<li>기술 스택: Python + prompt_toolkit + rich + tree-sitter + Git</li>
<li>핵심 차별점: RepoMap, edit 전략 다양성, git auto-com</li>
</ul>
